Japanese: 同日　某時刻 成歩堂法律事務所
English: June 21 Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: ‥‥今回の事件、ちっとも 事務所に帰ってこられないな。
English: This case has had me out of the office most of the time...

Japanese: 明日こそ、帰ってこよう。 もちろん、真宵ちゃんを助けて。
English: I can come back tomorrow. Helping Maya should be my top priority now.

Japanese: 観葉植物のチャーリーくん。
English: Charley. A quite decorative plant.

Japanese: 世話に手間がかかるけど、 そのぶん愛着がわいてきた。
English: He's a handful, but I've grown quite attached to the little fella.

Japanese: きっと千尋さんも、そんな 感じでかわいがっていたんだろう。
English: He also helps me to remember all the good times with Mia.

Japanese: ムズカシイ法律の本が あざ笑うように並んでいる。
English: Difficult-looking legal books stand in a formidable row. They mock me.

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば、うっすらと ホコリをかぶってるな。
English: ...Actually, I've neglected them for so long, a layer of dust has started to form.

Japanese: たまには‥‥せめて 読むフリだけでもしてみるか。
English: One of these days, I should try to build up the courage to read one... Maybe.

Japanese: 窓の向かいがわには、 巨大なビルがそびえている。
English: There is a giant building just outside the window.

Japanese: “ホテル・バンドー” ゴウカな高級ホテルだ。
English: It's the Gatewater Hotel, a high-class, luxury hotel.

Japanese: ちょっと前までは、ただの ビジネスホテルだったのに。
English: Until recently, it had been a normal, business-class hotel.

Japanese: ぼくのデスクだ。
English: It's my desk.

Japanese: あまり座る機会がないため、 いやにキチンとかたづいている。
English: I keep it neat for those rare occasions when I actually have a client in the office.

Japanese: テレビのヒーロー “トノサマン”のポスター。
English: A poster of the TV action hero, the Steel Samurai.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんがここを 去る前に張っていったものだ。
English: Maya stuck it up here right before she left.

Japanese: 弁護士事務所に“トノサマン”は どうかと思うんだけど、
English: I wouldn't say that the Steel Samurai and an attorney's office are a good match, but

Japanese: 『はがしたら呪われる』という コトバがコワくて、はがせない。
English: Maya threatened to curse me with some magic spell... so I guess it can stay.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: June 21 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんは‥‥ 取り調べ中ということだ）
English: (Maya must still be in questioning...)

Japanese: （もう少しで終わるそうだから、 他のこと、調べておくか‥‥）
English: (She'll probably be done in a little bit, so I'll come back later...)

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: タイクツなのだろうか。 こっちには見向きもしない。
English: I wonder if he's bored. Not that I can tell since he never looks over this way.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile, you're on candid camera.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: June 21 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: あっ、なるほどくん！ 待ってたよっ！
English: Hey, Nick... I've been waiting...

Japanese: （‥‥よっぽど 心細かったみたいだな）
English: (...She looks so helpless...)

Japanese: どう？　どう？　調子は。 ナニかわかった！
English: So? So? How was it!? What did you find out!?

Japanese: ‥‥う、うん。 まあ、ぼちぼちと‥‥。
English: Um... Well... I found a few things...

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん、 目が泳いでるよ‥‥
English: My eyes are tearing up, Nick. Don't make me cry...

Japanese: も、もうちょっと時間がほしい。 明日までにはなんとかするから。
English: I... I just need a little more time. It'll all come together by tomorrow, I promise.

Japanese: ‥‥ごめんね。会うたびに メイワクばっかり‥‥。
English: I'm sorry, Nick. Every time I see you, all I do is cause trouble...

Japanese: どうだった？　今日の裁判は。
English: So how was it? Today's trial, I mean...

Japanese: さすがなるほどくん！ って感じだったよね。
English: You were fantastic, Nick!

Japanese: もうダメ！　‥‥って思ったら、 いつのまにか逆転してるの。
English: Every time I thought our goose was cooked, you managed to turn it all around.

Japanese: ‥‥演出？　あれ。
English: I bet it would make a great Hollywood movie!

Japanese: バカ言うなよ。
English: Don't be silly.

Japanese: 法廷が終わるたび、イノチが 音をたてて縮んでいくよ。
English: We have to concentrate on the trial. We're going to win this, Maya!

Japanese: ‥‥ホント、ありがとう。
English: ...Thanks a lot. Really.

Japanese: ね。なるほどくん？
English: Hey, Nick?

Japanese: 霧崎先生を撃ったの、 あたしじゃないんだよね？
English: The person who shot Dr. Grey wasn't me, was it?

Japanese: そうだよ。きみは完全に無実だ。
English: Of course not. You're completely innocent.

Japanese: ホントにそうなら、 うれしいよあたし。でも‥‥
English: If that were true, then I'd be really happy, but...

Japanese: あの部屋には、あたしと 霧崎先生しかいなかったんだよ？
English: there wasn't anyone else in that room with the two of us, right?

Japanese: ビョウブのうしろは？　見た？
English: Well, did you check behind the folding screen?

Japanese: うーん‥‥チラッとだけど。 人はいなかったなあ。
English: Um... I took a quick glance, but there wasn't anyone there.

Japanese: ‥‥ううん‥‥。 （ナツミさんもそう言ってたな）
English: Hmm... (That's exactly what Lotta said too...)

Japanese: あのさ。なるほどくん。
English: Um, Nick...

Japanese: ん？
English: Yeah?

Japanese: なるほどくんは、 どうして信じてくれるの？
English: Why do you believe in me?

Japanese: あたしが霧崎先生を 殺してない、って。
English: Why do you think I didn't kill Dr. Grey?

Japanese: ‥‥千尋さんが 教えてくれたんだ。
English: ...Because of something Mia told me.

Japanese: お姉ちゃんが？
English: My sis? What did she tell you?

Japanese: “霊媒師は、夢を見ない”って。
English: She said that spirit mediums can't have dreams.

Japanese: 霊媒中は意識がとぎれるから、 夢なんか見られるハズがない。
English: When you're channeling, it's impossible for you to dream because your soul leaves you.

Japanese: あ‥‥そういえば。 じゃ、じゃあ、あたし‥‥？
English: Now that you mention it... Wait, then... then... I really am...?

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんは、犯人じゃない。
English: You are not the real murderer.

Japanese: たぶん‥‥霊を呼ぶ前に、 眠らされたんじゃないかな。
English: I think that... maybe before you even called the spirit, you were drugged.

Japanese: 眠らされた‥‥？
English: Drugged...?

Japanese: 最初から、計画されて いたのかもしれない。
English: I think this was planned from the very beginning.

Japanese: 霧崎先生が死ぬことも、 真宵ちゃんに罪を着せることも。
English: And you were set up to take the blame for killing Dr. Grey.
That's... That's...

Japanese: 春美ちゃんのことなんだけど。
English: I want to ask you something about Pearls.

Japanese: ‥‥はみちゃん？
English: Pearly? What about her?

Japanese: あの子、どうしても 教えてくれないんだよ。
English: No matter what I try, she won't answer me.

Japanese: 事件があったとき、 どこで何をしていたか。
English: I want to know what she was doing at the time of the murder.
Hmm...

Japanese: な、なるほどくん！ は、はみちゃんを疑ってるの？
English: W-Wait! Nick! Y-You don't think she did it, do you!?

Japanese: いや、そういうわけじゃ‥‥
English: No, no, nothing like that...

Japanese: 見そこなったよっ！ いい？　はみちゃんはね。
English: She'd never do anything like that! You hear me!? Never!

Japanese: とってもイイ子で、かわいくて、 イイ子で、ええと‥‥かわいくて。
English: She's a great kid, and really cute... And really great... And cute.

Japanese: あの子が犯人だなんて 思ってないよ。
English: I didn't really think she was the murderer.

Japanese: ただ‥‥ 何かかくしてるんだ。
English: But... she's definitely hiding something.

Japanese: ううう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: あの日、真宵ちゃんは あの子といっしょだったよね？
English: You were with her on that day, right?

Japanese: うん‥‥２人でね。 マリつきしてあそんでたの。
English: Yeah. The two of us. We were playing with her ball.

Japanese: マリつき‥‥ねえ。 （手がかりにはならないかな）
English: A ball, huh? (Sounds like a dead-end to me...)

Japanese: ‥‥そのマリについて、 もうちょっと聞かせてよ。
English: So, about this ball...

Japanese: ‥‥え。そう言われても‥‥ ふつうのマリだよ。
English: It's nothing really special... Just a plain old ball.

Japanese: あたしが霊媒のジュンビを始めて からも、あそんでたみたい。
English: I think she was still playing with it when I started the channeling job that day.

Japanese: はみちゃん、 マリ大好きっ子だから。
English: Pearly really loves to play with that ball.

Japanese: “大好きっ子”‥‥。
English: She "loves to play with that ball", huh...?

Japanese: マリが見たいんだったら、 衣装ばこの中に入ってると思うよ。
English: When she wants to get it, she always climbs right into the clothing box.

Japanese: 衣装ばこ？
English: The clothing box?

Japanese: うん。はみちゃん、 いつもあそこにしまってるんだ。
English: Yeah. Pearly's always hiding in there.

Japanese: あのね。“控えの間”にある 衣装ばこ。
English: You know the Side Room? There's a clothing box in there.

Japanese: おっきいから、 すぐわかると思うよ。
English: It's really big, so once you see it, I'm sure you'll know what I'm talking about.

Japanese: （“控えの間”の 衣装ばこ、か‥‥）
English: (The clothing box in the Side Room, huh?)

Japanese: あいかわらず、それ 見せるの好きだね、なるほどくん。
English: It's the same as the last time I saw it. You just like to show it off, don't you, Nick?

Japanese: そんなにうれしいの？ 弁護士になれて。
English: Are you that thrilled to be a lawyer?

Japanese: そういうわけじゃ ないけど‥‥。
English: Th-That's not what I meant by it, but... I mean, I don't really think about it.

Japanese: 気がついたらつきつけてるんだ。 クセみたいなものかな。
English: I just sort of shove it in people's faces... Force of habit, I guess.

Japanese: その穴‥‥ やっぱり、ピストルで？
English: That hole... It looks an awful lot like a bullet hole...

Japanese: ‥‥たぶんね。
English: That's probably what it is.

Japanese: ホント、ヒドいよね！ ‥‥大事なビョウブなのに！
English: That's TERRIBLE! How could this happen to such an important screen!?

Japanese: いやいや、それより、 弾丸が当たらなくてよかったね。
English: Um, instead of dwelling on the hole, let's focus on the fact that you weren't shot...

Japanese: うわ、そうか！ 撃たれたの、あたしなんだ‥‥。
English: Hey, you're right! So I was the one who was about to get shot, huh?

Japanese: ヒ、ヒドいよねッ！ あたしを撃つなんてさッ！
English: Th-That's TERRIBLE! Who'd want to shoot little ol' me!?

Japanese: （今まで気づいて なかったのかな‥‥）
English: (I guess she didn't realize she was the one being shot at until now...)

Japanese: ‥‥ウチの台所に あったヤツだよ、それ。
English: That's a knife from my family's kitchen.

Japanese: もう使えないね。ナイフ。
English: I'll never be able to use it again...

Japanese: だいじょうぶだよ。新しいナイフ 買ってあげるから。
English: It's alright. I'll buy you a new one, OK?

Japanese: もう！　そうじゃないよ！
English: Argh! That's not the point!

Japanese: ‥‥ナイフなんて、二度と 見たくない‥‥。
English: ...I don't ever want to see another knife... ever again...

Japanese: そ‥‥それ‥‥。 やっぱり、あたしの‥‥。
English: That... That's... That's mine, isn't it?

Japanese: （しまった！　また うつむいちまったぞ‥‥）
English: (Dammit! I upset her again...)

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥。あたし、 ホントにやってないのかな？
English: It really was me, wasn't it? Wasn't it, Nick?

Japanese: これ、あたし‥‥なんだよね。
English: This... This is me, right?

Japanese: やっぱり、あたしが撃ったんだ。 霧崎先生のコト‥‥
English: I guess I really did shoot Dr. Grey...

Japanese: そんなことないよ。
English: But that's not you!

Japanese: そんなことなくないッ！
English: Is too!

Japanese: そんなことなくなくない！
English: Is not!

Japanese: そんなことなくなくなくなくなく
English: Is too, is too!

Japanese: （‥‥最初から、こんな写真 見せるんじゃなかったな‥‥）
English: (I should've kept this picture to myself...)

Japanese: それ、倉院のツボだよ。
English: That's Kurain's Sacred Urn.

Japanese: キョウコさまのタマシイが 封じこめられているの。
English: Mystic Ami's soul is sealed inside of it.

Japanese: それなら知ってるよ。 創始者なんでしょ。霊媒の。
English: Yeah, I know all about it. About how she's the founder of the Kurain Technique.

Japanese: お。なかなかの マニアっぷりだねー。
English: Ooh, look at Mr. Smarty here! Thinks he knows all! You sound like an occult fanatic.

Japanese: （‥‥うれしくない‥‥）
English: (...Trust me, this isn't going on my resume any time soon...)

Japanese: でも、しばらく見ないうちに、 ずいぶんボロっちくなったなあ。
English: You know, I haven't looked at this in a long time. I wonder when it became so cracked?

Japanese: ‥‥なあに、これ。
English: ...What is it?

Japanese: さあね。さっき、 焼却炉で見つけたんだ。
English: I'm not too sure myself, but I found it just now inside the furnace.

Japanese: あれ？　今日って、 ゴミを燃やす日だったっけ？
English: Huh? Is it trash burning day already?

Japanese: そ、そんなの ぼくが知るかよ。
English: That's not something I would know about...

Japanese: （血のあとには 気づいてないみたいだな。）
English: (I guess she didn't notice the bloodstain.)

Japanese: （そっとしておくか‥‥）
English: (Probably better that way...)

Japanese: はみちゃんのたん生日に、 あたしがプレゼントしたんだよ。
English: I gave that to her on her birthday.

Japanese: 大江戸戦士トノサマンの 絵がプリントされてるの。
English: It's got the Steel Samurai on it, you know.

Japanese: ヤングにバカウケなんだよ！
English: It's a hit with the kids!

Japanese: （ジマンげにマリを 見せられてもなあ‥‥）
English: (If she puffs up any more, the peacock at the zoo's going to feel threatened...)

Japanese: あ。それ、《対面の間》の！
English: Ah! That's... The Channeling Chamber's...

Japanese: これって、 １つしかないんだよね？
English: This is the only one in the world, right?

Japanese: そうだよ。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: あたしもフシギ。なんで なるほどくんが持ってるの？
English: During the trial, I was kinda wondering why you had it...

Japanese: 春美ちゃんからもらったんだ。
English: I got it from Pearls.

Japanese: おっかしいな‥‥。
English: That's weird...

Japanese: あたし、扉にカギかけて‥‥、 たもとの中に入れておいたのに。
English: I swear I locked the door... and then put the key into one of my pouchy sleeves...

Japanese: たもとの中‥‥。
English: Your sleeve?

Japanese: それがいつの間にか、 あの部屋から出ていたワケだ。
English: But how and when would your sleeve leave the room? It didn't grow a pair of legs...

Japanese: はみちゃん‥‥ どこでひろったんだろうね、それ。
English: I wonder where Pearly found that key...

Japanese: ‥‥うーん‥‥。 特に、何も思いつかないよ。
English: Um, I can't think of anything special about it...

Japanese: 取り調べのとき‥‥ ツラかったんだよ。
English: I felt terrible during the questioning session...

Japanese: だって‥‥イトノコさん、 あたしのこと‥‥
English: I mean, Gumshoe kept looking at me...

Japanese: とっても悲しそうな目で 見るんだもん‥‥。
English: with these really sad eyes...

Japanese: ！！ ‥‥あ、あたしが‥‥こ、ころし
English: T-The... That's the guy I k-killed...

Japanese: あ‥‥ご、ごめん！ （この写真は見せちゃダメだろ！）
English: Ack! I'm sorry! (Why did I pull this picture out!?)

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり、あたしなんかより、 はみちゃんが‥‥
English: Pearly... She definitely has more than me...

Japanese: ？　‥‥なんのこと？
English: Huh? What are you talking about?

Japanese: 家元。‥‥きっと、 あたしより霊力も強いし、
English: Who I think the next Master should be... I mean, she's got more spiritual power than me,

Japanese: 修行だって キチンとやってるし‥‥。
English: and she really works hard at her training...

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ。かなり 落ちこんでるな‥‥）
English: (Way to go Phoenix... Way to bring the mood down even further... *sigh*)

Japanese: ‥‥見たくない。 あたしのカオなんて‥‥。
English: I... I can't face myself...

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥）
English: (I should've known better...)

Japanese: おねえちゃん‥‥ あたし、どうしたら！
English: What am I supposed to do, sis!?

Japanese: ナツミさん‥‥だよね。
English: That's Lotta Hart... right?

Japanese: 彼女、またよけいな写真 撮ってくれちゃってさ。
English: That girl is still after the next sensational photo.

Japanese: ふふ‥‥。 あいかわらずだね。
English: Ha ha. I guess some things never change.

Japanese: おばさま‥‥。あたし、 カオ見せられないよ。
English: I... I can't face my aunt right now...

Japanese: こ、こんな事件、 起こしちゃって‥‥
English: Not now... that this has happened...

Japanese: だいじょうぶだよ。 彼女、真宵ちゃんの味方だから。
English: It's alright, Maya. I'm sure she understands. She's on our side, after all.

Japanese: うん‥‥。 それはわかってるけど‥‥
English: Yeah, I know... But still...

Japanese: かるま検事のムスメさん ‥‥なんだよね。
English: So she's Prosecutor von Karma's daughter, huh?

Japanese: そうみたいだね。
English: Looks like it.

Japanese: それなら‥‥ あの人のコトも知ってるのかな？
English: Well, then... she'd know about that person, right?

Japanese: （“あの人”って‥‥ アイツのこと、か‥‥）
English: ("That person"...? Is she talking about "him"?)

Japanese: ‥‥いいよ。 今は、そんな話‥‥
English: It's alright. We don't need to talk about that right now...

Japanese: ‥‥キョウコさま。 伝説の霊媒師だよ。
English: ...Mystic Ami. She's a Legendary Spirit Medium.

Japanese: いいよねー。 “デンセツ”ってヒビキ。
English: I like the way that word sounds... "Legendary"...

Japanese: あたしも小さいころ、 将来のユメは“伝説”だったね。
English: Ever since I was young, I've dreamed of being a "Legend".

Japanese: （今度の事件で、じゅうぶん “伝説”になれると思うけど）
English: (Well, this murder will make you into a "legend", alright...)

Japanese: （それは、言わない方が いいな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... I probably shouldn't say that last thought out loud...)

Japanese: あの‥‥このヒトのこと なんだけど‥‥
English: So... what do you think about this person...?

Japanese: （今、このヒトのことを 聞いても、しかたないな）
English: (I guess I can't expect her to know anything about this person.)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: June 21 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: ち‥‥千尋さん！
English: M-Mia!

Japanese: あ。来てくれたわね。
English: Phoenix, it's good to see you.

Japanese: どうしてもシンパイだったから、 出てきちゃった。
English: I was really worried so I thought I'd see how you were doing.

Japanese: ‥‥また、撮られてるかも しれませんよ、写真。
English: ...You might get caught on film again...

Japanese: バレちゃったものは しかたないでしょ。
English: There are certain risks you have to take in life, or in my case, death.

Japanese: それより、どう？ 何か手がかりは見つかった？
English: Anyway, how are you doing? Have you found anything useful?

Japanese: そうですね‥‥少しずつ、 わかってきたような気がします。
English: Um, only a little. I think I'm slowly beginning to understand everything, piece by piece.

Japanese: ‥‥なんとか、明日には 決着をつけたいわね‥‥
English: Everything will resolve itself by tomorrow, somehow.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、私は そろそろ帰るわ。
English: Well, it's about time I left.

Japanese: ち、千尋さん！
English: M-Mia!

Japanese: 看守さんに見つかると ヤッカイだしね。
English: We'll be in big trouble if the guard sees me here...

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix...

Japanese: はい！
English: Yes, Chief!

Japanese: この事件には、まだまだ ウラがあるわ。
English: There is still a lot of dirt left in this case.

Japanese: 証拠がすべて集まった‥‥ そう思ったら、また来て。
English: Once you think you've gathered all the evidence, then come back.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: ...Yes, Chief.

Japanese: 明日の開廷までに、どうしても 知っておくべきことがあるわ。
English: There's still a whole slew of questions for you to answer before the trial tomorrow.

Japanese: 真犯人が、どうやってあの部屋に 入って霧崎先生を殺害したか‥‥？
English: You mean like how did the real murderer get into the room and kill Dr. Grey...?

Japanese: そうね。
English: Yes.

Japanese: そもそも、犯人はいつ《対面の間》 に入りこんだのか‥‥？
English: Also, when did the murderer enter the Channeling Chamber...?

Japanese: 霊媒が始まってからは、あなたたち が部屋の前にいたわ。
English: You guys were all standing around in front of the room after the channeling started.

Japanese: ということは、犯人は、霊媒が 始まる前から、あの部屋に‥‥？
English: Which means that the murderer must have gone into that room before the channeling...

Japanese: そう考えるのが自然ね。
English: That's what I think, in any case.

Japanese: でも、真宵ちゃんは “だれもいなかった”って‥‥
English: But Maya's already said there wasn't anyone else in there.
...That's true...

Japanese: とにかく、犯人の行動を立証 できなければ、勝ち目はない。
English: But if we can't figure out the murderer's movements, we're stuck like a beached whale.

Japanese: （ううん‥‥）
‥‥どうしたの？ 霊媒に興味を持ったのかしら。
English: I thought I'd never see this day. Are you actually asking me about spirit channeling?

Japanese: 今回の事件では、霊媒が “当然あるもの”になっています。
English: This case is all about spirit channeling.

Japanese: なんだか、どうも ナットクできなくて‥‥。
English: And I feel like I don't understand a thing about it...

Japanese: ‥‥それがフツウよ。
English: Well, that's only natural...

Japanese: “霊媒”なんて術は、日常の 近くにあるべきじゃないわ。
English: After all, spirit channeling isn't exactly an everyday thing, is it?

Japanese: そうなんでしょうね。
English: Yeah, I guess you're right.

Japanese: 生前の人のすがたに変身する力は、 とおい祖先が手に入れたの。
English: The power to change your physical appearance into the person you're calling

Japanese: アヤサト　キョウコさま っていうんだけど。
English: was something our ancestor, Mystic Ami Fey acquired.

Japanese: ‥‥その名前、聞きました。
English: I've heard that name before...

Japanese: アヤサトはもともと、神に仕える 血筋の家だったの。
English: The Fey family was originally a family known for its service to the gods.

Japanese: キョウコさまは、生まれた ときから“さだめ”があった。
English: When Mystic Ami was born, she was given a certain fate through her name.

Japanese: “神のもとに供えられる子” という“さだめ”。
English: Because, you see, she was named after one of the Buddhas of Buddhism, "Amitábha".

Japanese: 彼女の魂は、今もツボに 封じこめられているの。
English: To this day, her soul is still sealed in the Kurain Sacred Urn.

Japanese: ろうかにかざってあったでしょ？ 彼女の名前が書かれているわ。
English: It usually sits in the Winding Way, and has her name written on it.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: Um, yeah...

Japanese: そんなカオしないで。 ‥‥おとぎ話よ。
English: Aww, come on, Phoenix. It's just a fairy-tale.

Japanese: （千尋さんだって、 おとぎ話みたいだよな‥‥）
English: (Are you saying that me being able to see you is just a fairy-tale too...?)

Japanese: ‥‥ダメよ。私たちには、 ムダにできる時間はないの。
English: Come on, Phoenix. We don't have time to be looking at things like this.

Japanese: すべての材料が そろってからにしましょう‥‥。
English: We need to find out everything we can for the trial tomorrow.

Japanese: 同日　午後　６時３８分 留置所　面会室
English: June 21, 6:38 PM Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: 待っていたわ、なるほどくん。
English: You kept me waiting, Phoenix.

Japanese: 千尋さん‥‥！
English: Mia!

Japanese: どう？　明日の準備は。
English: So, how are the preparations?

Japanese: たぶん‥‥ 材料はそろったと思います。
English: I think I have enough evidence and information now... I think...

Japanese: この事件は‥‥ 明日、カタがつくわ。
English: Everything will be on your shoulders tomorrow.

Japanese: ‥‥無罪にしろ、 そうでないにしろ‥‥ね。
English: Whether Maya is found guilty or innocent... It's a heavy weight to bear.

Japanese: （ぼくはおぼえている‥‥）
（千尋さんのムネに、 ヒミツが‥‥）
English: (If memory serves...)

Japanese: （サイコ・ロックが かかっていることを！）
English: (Mia had a few Psyche-Locks of her own...)

Japanese: 千尋さん。‥‥最後の手がかりを いただきたいと思います。
English: Mia. I'm going to get the last piece of information I need.

Japanese: ‥‥いいわ。 うばってみなさい。私から。
English: Sure. I welcome you to try.

Japanese: 千尋さん‥‥。
English: Mia...

Japanese: なあに？
English: Yes?

Japanese: 千尋さんには‥‥ 心当たりがあるんですね？
English: Mia... You know something, don't you?

Japanese: この事件の真犯人に。
English: About the real murderer, I mean.

Japanese: どうして‥‥そう思うの？
English: Why... do you think so?

Japanese: （人のヒミツが見えるのも、 ツラいときがあるな‥‥）
English: (It's really trying at times to be able to see other people's secrets...)

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら‥‥あなたの 考えを聞くときが来たようね。
English: It looks like it's time for you to explain your reasoning.

Japanese: そのようですね‥‥。 （千尋さんと‥‥勝負だ！）
English: Yeah... (A battle of wits against Mia! ...*gulp*)

Japanese: 千尋さん。 真宵ちゃんを助けたいんです。
English: Mia, I want to help Maya any way I can.

Japanese: そのためには、真犯人を‥‥
English: And to do that, I need to know who the real murderer is...

Japanese: わかってる。 だから‥‥あなたの実力を見せて。
English: I know. Now, show me your ability as a lawyer.

Japanese: （千尋さんが口に出すのを ためらう“犯人”‥‥）
English: (This is it...)

Japanese: （‥‥今なら、 ハッキリわかるはずだ！）
English: (The real murderer that Mia's been hesitant to name all this time!)

Japanese: ～犯人について～
English: -- The Murderer --

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix...

Japanese: 私が、だれかの情報をかくしている ‥‥そう思っているんでしょう？
English: You think I'm hiding information about someone from you... Isn't that right?

Japanese: ‥‥そうです。
English: Yes.

Japanese: 聞かせて。私は、だれのことを かくしているのかしら？
English: Alright, then. Tell me who you think I'm protecting.

Japanese: ええと‥‥ この人じゃないかな、と。
English: Um, I think you're trying to protect this person...?

Japanese: なるほどくん。 私‥‥あなたの力を信用したいの。
English: Phoenix. Don't let me down like this.

Japanese: は、はい。
English: I... I'm sorry.

Japanese: 真宵の命がかかってるのよ。 もっと真剣になって！
English: Maya's life is on the line! Concentrate harder!

Japanese: （今日の千尋さん ‥‥キビシイな‥‥）
English: (Mia's especially strict today...)

Japanese: ‥‥どう？ 考えはまとまった？
English: So? Have you gathered your thoughts?

Japanese: もちろん、キミ子さんですよ。
English: Morgan Fey, of course.

Japanese: ‥‥千尋さんがかばおうとする 人物は、他にいません。
English: There is no one else you would protect like this, Mia...

Japanese: ‥‥ミゴトね。でも‥‥
English: Well done, but...

Japanese: おばさまは犯行の時間、 あなたといっしょにいたのよ。
English: While the murder was being committed, my aunt was outside with everyone else.

Japanese: これ以上たしかなアリバイはない。
English: Which means she already has an alibi.

Japanese: そうですね‥‥。
English: That's true...

Japanese: それならば、見せて。 おばさまがアヤシイという証拠を。
English: Show me some evidence that would cast suspicion onto my aunt.

Japanese: 証拠は‥‥これ、だと思います。
English: Um, I think this...?

Japanese: ダメ。ちがう。やりなおし。
English: No. You're wrong. Try again.

Japanese: ‥‥今日はヨウシャないですね、 千尋さん。
English: You're really unforgiving today, Mia...

Japanese: 何度も言わせないで。 真宵の命がかかってるのよ。
English: And I keep telling you... Don't take this lightly. Maya's life is on the line.

Japanese: （‥‥千尋さんも必死なんだ‥‥）
English: (Mia is really serious...)

Japanese: さあ。もう一度、聞くわ。
English: Now, I'm going to ask you one more time.

Japanese: そのコンキョは‥‥ この黒いカギです。
English: My evidence is this black key.

Japanese: このカギは、霊媒が始まるまで、 真宵ちゃんが持っていました。
English: At the beginning of the channeling, Maya had possession of this key.

Japanese: それがいつのまにか、庭の 焼却炉に移動していた。
English: And yet, somehow, it magically ended up in the incinerator in the center garden.

Japanese: ‥‥たしかにそうね。
English: ...You're right.

Japanese: 警察を呼びに行ってるあいだに、 それができたのは、１人だけ。
English: There is only one person who could have done that while I was busy calling the police.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんを介抱していた キミ子さんです。
English: The person taking care of Maya -- Morgan.

Japanese: どうですか？
English: What's that look mean?

Japanese: ‥‥それだけでは弱いわね。
English: Your argument is too flimsy.
Huh?

Japanese: そのカギはいつ、 あの焼却炉に移動したのかしら？
English: When did the key find its way into the incinerator?

Japanese: もしかしたら、事件が起こった ずっと後かもしれない。
English: It could have happened long after the murder happened.

Japanese: ずっと‥‥後？
English: Long after...?

Japanese: 落ちていたカギをだれかが拾って、 焼却炉に捨てたのかもしれないわ。
English: Someone could have found the key somewhere and dumped it into the incinerator.
But that's...

Japanese: 少なくとも、あの検事なら それぐらいは切り返してくるはず。
English: That prosecutor would use something this small to shred your case.

Japanese: そのカギの他に、ないかしら？ キミ子さんを示す証拠は？
English: Do you have something other than just the key to cast suspicion on Morgan?

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。 私たちにはもう、時間がないの。
English: Phoenix, we don't have any time left.

Japanese: （まだ何も言ってないのに‥‥）
English: (I haven't even said anything yet...)

Japanese: １つだけの証拠品で、おばさまを 犯人とは決めつけられないわ。
English: You can't pin my aunt as the murderer with one piece of evidence alone.

Japanese: キミ子さんを指し示す もう１つの証拠は、これです。
English: This is the other piece of evidence that points to Morgan as the culprit.

Japanese: これも、わたりろうかの 焼却炉から見つかりました。
English: I also found this in the garden incinerator.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんの装束と同じモノです。 ‥‥そして、少し血がついている。
English: It's the same material as Maya's costume... Except for the splotch of blood.

Japanese: でも、真宵のものとは かぎらないんじゃないかしら？
English: But this piece may not even be from Maya's costume.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんはこう言ってました。
English: But this is what Maya told me.

Japanese: 『霊媒中、カギは たもとに入れておいた』
English: "While I was channeling, the key was sitting inside my sleeve."

Japanese: ‥‥そのカギが、この布きれと いっしょに焼却炉から見つかった。
English: And both the key and this piece of cloth were found inside the incinerator.

Japanese: ということは、この布きれは、 真宵ちゃんの装束なんです。
English: Which means that this piece of cloth was part of Maya's costume.

Japanese: でも‥‥逮捕されたとき、 真宵はちゃんと装束を着ていたわ。
English: But when Maya was arrested, she was wearing her costume.

Japanese: 着せ替えられたんです。
English: That just means someone changed her clothes.

Japanese: 着せ替え‥‥。 いったい、なぜ？
English: Changed her clothes? But why...?

Japanese: それはわかりません。
English: I don't know why.

Japanese: でも‥‥それができた人物は、 たった１人です。
English: But... There is only one person who could've done that.

Japanese: 真宵を介抱していた人物‥‥。 おばさま、というわけ。
English: The one person who was taking care of Maya... My aunt.

Japanese: そのコンキョは‥‥ この布きれです。
English: The proof is this piece of cloth.

Japanese: わたりろうかの焼却炉で 見つけたものです。
English: I found it in the incinerator near the Winding Way.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんの装束と同じモノです。 ‥‥そして、少し血がついている。
English: It's the same material as Maya's costume... Except for the splotch of blood.

Japanese: つまり‥‥事件のとき、 真宵が着ていた装束だということ？
English: You mean, it's from Maya's costume that she was wearing at the time of the murder?

Japanese: そうです。
English: Yes.

Japanese: でも‥‥逮捕されたとき、 真宵はちゃんと装束を着ていたわ。
English: But when Maya was arrested, she was wearing her costume.

Japanese: 着せ替えられたんです。
English: That just means someone changed her clothes.

Japanese: 着せ替え‥‥。 いったい、なぜ？
English: Changed her clothes? But why...?

Japanese: それはわかりません。
English: I don't know why.

Japanese: でも‥‥それができた人物は、 たった１人です。
English: But... There is only one person who could've done that.

Japanese: 真宵を介抱していた人物‥‥。 おばさま、というわけね。
English: The one person who was taking care of Maya... My aunt.

Japanese: どうですか？
English: What's that look mean?

Japanese: ‥‥それだけでは弱いわね。
English: Your argument is too flimsy.
Huh?

Japanese: その布きれが真宵の装束だという 証拠がないわ。
English: You have no proof that this piece of cloth is actually from Maya's costume.

Japanese: でも‥‥こいつには、血が‥‥。
English: But... this blood...

Japanese: 今の時点では、だれの血だか わからないでしょう？
English: And do you know whose blood that is at this point in time? You don't, do you?

Japanese: その布きれが、真宵の装束だという 証拠はないのかしら‥‥？
English: Do you have any proof that piece of cloth is from Maya's costume?

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。 私たちにはもう、時間がないの。
English: Phoenix, we don't have any time left.

Japanese: （まだ何も言ってないのに‥‥）
English: (I haven't even said anything yet...)

Japanese: １つだけの証拠品で、おばさまを 犯人とは決めつけられないわ。
English: You can't pin my aunt as the murderer with one piece of evidence alone.

Japanese: その証拠は‥‥ これでしょうか。
English: I think this is the proof you're looking for.

Japanese: ‥‥《対面の間》のカギね。
English: ...The key to the Channeling Chamber, huh?

Japanese: このカギは、霊媒が始まるまで、 真宵ちゃんが持っていました。
English: At the beginning of the channeling, Maya had possession of this key.

Japanese: それがいつのまにか、庭の 焼却炉に移動していた。
English: And yet, somehow, it magically ended up in the incinerator in the center garden.

Japanese: ‥‥たしかにそうね。
English: ...You're right.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんはこう言ってました。
English: And this is what Maya told me.

Japanese: 『霊媒中、カギは たもとに入れておいた』
English: "While I was channeling, the key was sitting inside my sleeve."

Japanese: ‥‥そのカギが、この布きれと いっしょに焼却炉から見つかった。
English: And both this key and the piece of cloth were found inside the incinerator.

Japanese: ということは、この布きれは、 真宵ちゃんの装束なんです。
English: Which means that this piece of cloth was a part of Maya's costume.

Japanese: そういうことになるわね‥‥。
English: It would certainly seem that way...

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんは、別の装束に 着せ替えられたんです。
English: Someone changed her into a different set of clothes.

Japanese: そして、残った装束は、 焼却炉で焼かれた。
English: And then, burned the costume she was wearing in the incinerator.

Japanese: そんなことができたのは、 たった１人しかいません！
English: There is only one person who could've done that.

Japanese: 真宵を介抱していた人物‥‥。 おばさま、というわけ。
English: The one person who was taking care of Maya... My aunt.

Japanese: そのとおりです！
English: Exactly!

Japanese: ‥‥ミゴトね。なるほどくん‥‥。
English: You did very well, Phoenix.

Japanese: ありがとうございます。
English: Thank you.

Japanese: その２つの証拠があるかぎり、 おばさまは疑わしくなるわ。
English: As long as you have those two pieces of evidence, you can cast suspicion on my aunt.

Japanese: ‥‥でも。
English: However.

Japanese: で、でも‥‥？
English: H-However?

Japanese: 最初にも言ったとおり、 おばさまにはアリバイがあるわ。
English: Like I said in the beginning, my aunt has an alibi.

Japanese: ええ‥‥。キミ子さん、ぼくたちと いっしょにいましたからね。
English: Yeah... She was with us the whole time...

Japanese: 最後に聞かせて。 ‥‥確証はなくてもいいわ。
English: Here's my final question. You don't have to present solid proof of this for me.

Japanese: おばさまが犯人だというのなら、 その犯行の方法を教えて。
English: But I want to know. If my aunt is the culprit, then how did she commit the murder?

Japanese: 犯行の方法‥‥
English: How she did it, huh...?

Japanese: あなたの考えを聞きたいの。
English: I want to hear your thoughts.

Japanese: おばさまが、霧崎先生を殺害する ために必要だったのは‥‥？
English: In order for my aunt to murder Dr. Grey, who or what did she need...?

Japanese: （これは、いくらなんでも‥‥）
English: (No idea... Just throwing things out now...)

Japanese: ‥‥あと一息なの。 なるほどくん。
English: ...And you were so close too, Phoenix.
Yeah...

Japanese: おばさま自身には、 犯行は不可能だった。
English: There is no way my aunt could have done it herself.

Japanese: それなら、どうなの？ ‥‥カンタンなことでしょう？
English: So, thinking logically, where does that leave you? Should be easy for you to figure out...

Japanese: （キミ子さん自身には‥‥ 犯行は不可能‥‥！）
English: (So, if Morgan couldn't have done it herself... Then...)

Japanese: がんばって‥‥よく考えて。
English: Come on, Phoenix. Think.

Japanese: ‥‥葉中　のどか。 彼女こそ、最後の答えです。
English: ...Ini Miney. She's the answer to the final question.

Japanese: つまり、おばさまと のどかさんは‥‥？
English: Which means that my aunt and Ini Miney...

Japanese: 共犯だったんですね！
English: ...Were in cahoots!

Japanese: ６月２１日　午後　３時２４分 倉院の里
English: June 21, 3:24 PM Kurain Village

Japanese: わざわざ送っていただいて、 ありがとうございます。
English: Thank you very much for bringing me home, Mr. Nick.

Japanese: ついでだから。今日もいろいろ 調べなきゃならないし。
English: You're welcome. I had to come and look into a few things here today anyway.

Japanese: ‥‥あの。 ちょっと考えたんです。
English: Um... I thought about this a little.

Japanese: 真宵さまが、あの殺人を おやりになってないのなら‥‥
English: If Mystic Maya didn't kill that nice man...

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんは おやりになってないよ！
English: She definitely didn't!

Japanese: ええ、もちろんそうです。
English: Yes, of course.

Japanese: でも、そうなると‥‥ 他にいるわけですよね？
English: But if she didn't... Then someone else did, right?

Japanese: その‥‥“犯人”が！
English: Um, that "murderer" person!

Japanese: そうなるね。
English: Yes, Pearls.

Japanese: ‥‥そう‥‥ですよね‥‥。
English: Oh... I see...

Japanese: （どうしたんだ？ 考えこんじゃったぞ‥‥）
English: (I wonder what's wrong... She seems sad...)

Japanese: 巨大な岩が 天をニラみつけている。
English: A giant rock that seems to be reaching for the sky.

Japanese: 古ぼけた立て札に、 由来なんかが書いてあるな。
English: Its origins are written on a beat-up, old sign.

Japanese: “倉院の岩座（いわくら）” ‥‥と呼ばれている巨石らしい。
English: It looks like this giant rock is called the "Kurain Boulder".

Japanese: ムズカシイことが つらつら書かれているが‥‥、
English: There's a really complex explanation written here, but long story short,

Japanese: はやい話が、霊魂の宿る岩、 ということらしい。‥‥たぶん。
English: it's a big stone for spirits to live in... Or something like that...

Japanese: 古びたバス停だ。 “倉院の里”と書かれている。
English: An old bus stop. It says "Kurain Village" on the sign post.

Japanese: バスが‥‥朝、昼、晩の３本か。 やや不便な気がする。
English: The buses seem incredibly inconvenient since they only come 3 times a day.

Japanese: この風景にはなじまないが、 古いタイプの電話ボックスだ。
English: It's an old-style phone booth. I can't say it fits in with this area too well.

Japanese: 警察へは、この電話から 通報したんだよな。
English: I called the police from this phone.

Japanese: ここから見るかぎり、一番 リッパで大きなお屋敷だ。
English: The biggest, most elegant of the buildings I can see from here.

Japanese: これが綾里家、真宵ちゃんの 住んでいる屋敷らしい。
English: This is Fey Manor; the place Maya calls home.

Japanese: 思わず圧倒されそうになるな‥‥。
English: It's easy to be overwhelmed by its presence...

Japanese: ワラぶきの、かわいらしい 民家が並んでいる。
English: Rustic, thatch-roofed houses where the other villagers live line the street.

Japanese: 手前にある、お屋敷のような ハクリョクはない。
English: They're nowhere near as large or as nice as the manor in front of me.

Japanese: ‥‥それにしても、人のすがたが 見あたらないな‥‥。
English: It's kind of odd that I don't see any of the other villagers walking around, though...

Japanese: どうだった？ はじめての裁判所は？
English: So, what did you think of seeing your first trial?

Japanese: もう、ビックリです。
English: I was really surprised!

Japanese: あんなにたくさんの人を 見るの、はじめてでしたから。
English: I had never seen so many people before!

Japanese: そ、そうなんだ。
English: O-Oh...

Japanese: 真宵さまが、とても小さく見えて ‥‥おかわいそうでした。
English: And Mystic Maya looked so small... and helpless.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん、ジッサイに 小さいからなあ‥‥）
English: (Well, Maya's already naturally short, so...)

Japanese: それに、あのヒラヒラの服を着た、 キザっぽい女のかた。
English: And then, that snobby woman with the ruffly clothes...

Japanese: （ああ、狩魔検事のコトか‥‥）
English: (Ruffly... Oh, she means Franziska...)

Japanese: なんですか、あのタイドは！
English: She had such a bad attitude!

Japanese: あたかも“真宵さまがやった”って 決めつけてるみたいに‥‥
English: She acted like she had already decided that Mystic Maya did it...

Japanese: わたくし、明日こそ ひっぱたいてやりますとも！
English: I am going to tell her what I think of her tomorrow!

Japanese: （明日の法廷は 見のがせないな‥‥）
English: (I wouldn't miss tomorrow's trial for the world...)

Japanese: どうして、あの女のかたは 真宵さまをいじめるのですか？
English: Why was that woman bullying Mystic Maya?

Japanese: 弁護士とは逆に、被告の犯行を 立証するのが検事なんだよ。
English: Well, she's the opposite of a lawyer -- a prosecutor, so her job is to prove people guilty.

Japanese: 被告が無実だろうとかまわない。 自分の手がらしか考えてないんだ。
English: They don't care if someone's innocent. All they care about is if they win.

Japanese: そ、そんな‥‥！
English: Th-That's terrible!

Japanese: で、でも！　‥‥中には、 いい検事さんだっていますよね？
English: B-But... She's really a good prosecutor with a heart on the inside, right?

Japanese: そんなヤツ、いないよ。 検事なんて、みんなおなじさ。
English: There's no one like that. All prosecutors are the same.

Japanese: アイツだって‥‥結局 そうだったんだ‥‥。
English: Well, "he" might have become a good guy... Eventually...

Japanese: “アイツ”‥‥というのは どなたですか？
English: "He"? Who are you talking about, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: 真宵さまのおっしゃっていた、 ‥‥みつるぎ検事さん‥‥？
English: Is it that person Mystic Maya was talking about? Mr. Eh-ji-werth...?

Japanese: ‥‥‥ムカシの話だよ。 今はもう、いないんだ。
English: ...That was a long time ago. He's not around anymore...
Huh?

Japanese: つまらないプライドを抱いて ‥‥死んだよ。
English: He clutched onto his foolish pride too fiercely... and died for it.

Japanese: そ‥‥そうなのですか‥‥。
English: O-Oh... That's too bad...

Japanese: ところでさ。
English: By the way...

Japanese: はい。 なんでしょうか？
English: Yes? What is it?

Japanese: 春美ちゃん、心当たりはない？ ‥‥この事件の犯人。
English: Um, you wouldn't happen to know anything about who the murderer is... would you?

Japanese: な、な、何を おっしゃるんですか！
English: Wh-Why are you asking me!?

Japanese: いや‥‥。さっき、 考えこんでいたから‥‥。
English: No, I was just thinking earlier...

Japanese: わたくし、なにも 見ておりませんから！
English: I didn't see anything!

Japanese: （“何も見ていない”‥‥か）
English: (She "didn't see anything", huh...?)

Japanese: （そういえば、事件が あったとき‥‥）
English: (Speaking of that... During the murder...)

Japanese: （春美ちゃん、どこで 何をしてたのかな‥‥？）
English: (where was Pearls, and what was she doing...?)

Japanese: （それに、このカギ‥‥）
English: (And on top of that... This key...)

Japanese: （いったい、いつ拾ったんだ？ こんなモノ‥‥）
English: (Just when did she pick this up...?)

Japanese: そういえば、事件があったときの こと、聞いてなかったよね。
English: I just realized that I never asked you what you were doing at the time of the crime.

Japanese: 春美ちゃん、どこで何をしてたの？ 霊媒があったころ。
English: Pearls, where were you and what were you doing when the murder happened?

Japanese: わた、わた、わたくしごときモノの コトなんて、
English: Wh-What I-I w-was doing?

Japanese: どどどどどどどうでもよろしいじゃ ありませんか！
English: Ah, I-I w-wasn't d-doing a-anything!

Japanese: （かくしゴトは ニガテみたいだな‥‥）
English: (She's really bad at lying...)

Japanese: コッソリ、教えてよ。 だれにも言わないから。
English: You can whisper it to me. I won't tell anyone else. I promise.

Japanese: うわっ！
English: Aah!

Japanese: あの。もしかして、 見えちゃっているのですか？
English: Um... I guess you can see through my lie, huh?

Japanese: うん‥‥。ハッキリと。
English: Yeah... Clearly.

Japanese: ‥‥ウソ、つけませんね。 なるほどくんには‥‥。
English: I can't lie to you, can I, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: ぼくにも話せないの？ 事件があったときのこと。
English: You really won't tell me what you were doing at the time of the murder...?

Japanese: ‥‥す、すみません。
English: I'm sorry...

Japanese: でも、この勾玉があれば、 ロックを解除できるんでしょ？
English: But with the Magatama, I can unlock your Psyche-Locks, right?

Japanese: そ、そういうことになります。 ‥‥ああ、どうしましょう。
English: Yes... Ah, what should I do...?

Japanese: わたくしのヒミツを、 知られてしまうのでしょうか‥‥。
English: Should I tell you what my secret is...?

Japanese: （‥‥すんなり 教えてくれればいいのに）
English: (If you would just tell me, it would be easier for the both of us...)

Japanese: すごく、どきどきします。
English: I'm so nervous...

Japanese: （春美ちゃんは 事件のカギをにぎっている！）
English: (Pearls is holding the key to this whole case! I can feel it!)

Japanese: （‥‥そういえば、こっちのカギの コトも気になる‥‥）
English: (...Speaking of keys, this one turned out to be quite important too...)

Japanese: （まさに、今日の法廷の “カギ”だったもんな）
English: (I didn't think it would literally be the "key" to today's trial.)

Japanese: ‥‥それが、ベンゴシさんの 秘宝なのですか？
English: So... that's a lawyer's Sacred Treasure?
Huh?

Japanese: きっと、わたくしどもの 勾玉のようなフシギなチカラが‥‥
English: I feel like it has a mysterious power, just like my Magatama...

Japanese: （うーん。こういう場合、ユメを コワさないほうがいいのかな）
English: (Hmm, well, I guess I can let her think what she wants on this one...)

Japanese: ‥‥まあ。なるほどくんも こういうものに興味が？
English: So you like things about spirit channeling too?

Japanese: 倉院流霊媒道の呪詞が 書かれているのですよ。
English: The Kurain Sacred Writings are on this folding screen.

Japanese: へえ。そうなんだ。
English: Oh? Is that what's written on it?

Japanese: 呪詞は、ぜんぶで６つあります。
English: There are six lines to the Sacred Writings.

Japanese: 自らの魂を清める‥‥ 霊界の扉をひらく‥‥
English: Cleanse thy soul... Open the door to the spiritual realm beyond...

Japanese: 霊魂と語らう‥‥ 自らに呪いをかける‥‥
English: Converse with the departed...

Japanese: その呪いを律する‥‥ その呪いをはらう‥‥
English: Cast on thyself Protection... Maintain the spell... Release the spell...

Japanese: （‥‥とりあえず、事件には 関係なさそうでよかった‥‥）
English: (So basically, this has nothing to do with the murder... Well, that's super.)

Japanese: 春美ちゃん。 これ、なんだけど‥‥。
English: About this key, Pearls...

Japanese: 事件があった日に、 ひろったんだよね？
English: You found it on the day of the murder, right?

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: Yes.

Japanese: このカギがどこにあったか、 すごく重要なんだ。
English: Now, where you found this key is very important.

Japanese: 教えてくれないかな。 春美ちゃん、どこで見つけたの？
English: So, please tell me, Pearl. Where did you find this?

Japanese: あの。‥‥焼却炉で‥‥。
English: Um... I found it in the incinerator...

Japanese: しょうきゃくろ？ （庭にあったやつか‥‥）
English: The incinerator? (Hmm, is she talking about the one in the garden?)

Japanese: フタがあいていたので、 ちょっとのぞいてみたら‥‥
English: The door on it was a little open, so I took a peek...

Japanese: それって、いつのこと？
English: When was that?

Japanese: 霊媒が終わって、みなさんが 大さわぎしていたころです。
English: It was after the channeling, when everyone was in a panic.

Japanese: ふうん‥‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: あの‥‥。 なるほどくん？
English: Um... Mr. Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥？　どうしたの？
English: Yes?

Japanese: おかあさまにはナイショに しておいていただけますか？
English: Are you going to tell my mother?

Japanese: え‥‥？　キミ子さんに？
English: Huh? You mean, tell Morgan what you did?

Japanese: はい。焼け残ったゴミを ひろった、なんてバレたら、
English: Yes. If she finds out that I was playing in the left-over trash,

Japanese: おしりをたたかれてしまいます。
English: I'll get a spanking.

Japanese: ‥‥わかった。キミ子さんには ヒミツにしておくよ。
English: ...Ah, gotcha. Well, let's keep it a secret from her then, alright?

Japanese: （焼却炉、か‥‥ 調べてみたほうがいいかな）
English: (Hmm, the incinerator, huh? Sounds like a good place to search next...)

Japanese: そのナイフなら、 見たことがあります。
English: I've seen this knife before.

Japanese: え！　どこで！
English: Really!? Where!?

Japanese: お‥‥お台所ですけど‥‥
English: M-My kitchen...

Japanese: （そうか‥‥。このナイフ、 屋敷のものだったっけ）
English: (Oh... That's right... This knife belongs to the Feys...)

Japanese: あとで、おリンゴむいて あげますね。
English: I'll skin and cut up some apples for you later, Mr. Nick!

Japanese: う、うん。‥‥ありがと。
English: O-OK... Thanks.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥真宵さまの！
English: Th-That's Mystic Maya's!

Japanese: （しまった！　シゲキが 強すぎたかな‥‥）
English: (Ack!! I shouldn't have shown her something this violent...)

Japanese: まま、まさか、それは‥‥ ち、血なのですかッ！
English: I-I-Is that... Blood!?

Japanese: い、いや、いいや。 ごめんね。
English: Umm... It's nothing. Really. Just forget your ever saw this, okay?

Japanese: （相手はまだ小さい女の子だ。 気をつけよう‥‥）
English: (What was I thinking? I can't show stuff like this to a child.)

Japanese: （さすがにこの写真を 見せるのはまずいだろう）
English: (On second thought, showing her this picture might cause severe trauma...)

Japanese: 何かお役に立てれば いいのですが、
English: I'm sure it is helpful to you in some way,

Japanese: なにぶん今は修行中の身で、 特に思いつくことは‥‥。
English: but I'm still in training, so I don't know anything about it.

Japanese: 本当に、ほんとうに もうしわけございません。
English: I'm really, deeply sorry.

Japanese: い、いえいえ。
English: No, no, no, it's OK.

Japanese: こちらこそ、ツマラナイ ものを見せちゃって‥‥
English: I shouldn't have shown you something so trivial anyway.

Japanese: どうもスミマセン。 （‥‥て、何かオカシイ気がする）
English: I'm very sorry. (This feels so weird, talking to a kid like this...)

Japanese: あ。おかあさま。
English: Oh, it's my mother!

Japanese: わたくしを、とても大切に してくださるのですよ。
English: She takes really good care of me and loves me very much.

Japanese: 《ぶんけのほこりだ》と言って。 ‥‥意味はわかりませんけれど。
English: She says I'm the "pride of the branch family"... But I don't know what that means.

Japanese: （《分家》‥‥か。春美ちゃんには まだ、ムズカシイかな）
English: ("Branch family"... That concept is probably still too hard for little Pearl...)

Japanese: わたくしも、真宵さまのように 髪を長くしたいのですが、
English: To tell you the truth, I really want to grow my hair out, just like Mystic Maya.

Japanese: おかあさまが ゆるしてくれないのです。
English: But my mother wouldn't let me.

Japanese: でも、その髪型も にあってるよ。
English: I think your hair now suits you perfectly.

Japanese: そそ、そうですか。 ああ、ありがとうございます。
English: R-Really? Th-Thank you!

Japanese: ‥‥もじもじ。
English: *fidget, fidget*

Japanese: ‥‥もじもじ。
English: *fidget, fidget*

Japanese: 真宵さまは、わたくしの アコガレです。
English: I look up to Mystic Maya a lot. She's my role model.

Japanese: あたまがよくて、うつくしくて、 やさしくて、あたたかくて、
English: She's so smart, and pretty, and kind, and loving.

Japanese: いつもほがらかで、カゼもひかず、 好きキライもせず、早寝早起き、
English: She's always cheerful, never gets sick, isn't picky, and has good sleeping habits.

Japanese: わたくしより、かけっこも早いし、 ごはんを食べるのも早いし、
English: She always wakes up before me, and always eats breakfast before me, too.

Japanese: ‥‥それに、こんなステキな 殿方と、オトナの
English: And she has you; such a wonderful person with whom she can share a lo--

Japanese: わー！　よくわかったよ。 （よっぽど好きなんだな‥‥）
English: Ack! OK, OK! I get the idea! (She really thinks the world of Maya, doesn't she?)

Japanese: わたくし、ちっとも 知りませんでした。
English: I knew her for a little while.

Japanese: 千尋さまが、なるほどくんの お師匠さまだった、なんて。
English: Mystic Mia was your teacher, right?

Japanese: きっと、かなりの 使い手だったのでしょうね。
English: I'm sure she was a great Master of Lawyers.
("Master of Lawyers"... I guess...? I mean, it's not exactly a martial art...)

Japanese: なるほどくんも、なまけては ダメですよ。修行。
English: You should keep training, Mr. Nick.

Japanese: う‥‥うん。
English: Y-Yeah, you're probably right.

Japanese: あっ！　このひと！
English: Ah! This person!

Japanese: ユルせません！ 真宵さまをいじめるなんて！
English: I can't let her get away with bullying Mystic Maya like that!

Japanese: ええもうわたくし、 呪ってやりますとも！
English: I'm going to cast a curse on her!

Japanese: れ、霊媒師って、そんなコトも できるんだ‥‥。
English: C-Can spirit mediums really do that...?

Japanese: ムリでした。よく考えたら。
English: Actually, no, I guess not...

Japanese: ‥‥ううう、 どうしてくれましょう‥‥
English: Grr... I have to find something I can do, then!

Japanese: （綾里家の人たちは 怒らせるとコワいな‥‥）
English: (When they're mad, the women of the Fey family could give Medusa a run for her money...)

Japanese: あまり見かけない おカオですね。
English: Hmm, I'm sorry I don't know who this person is.

Japanese: この里の人じゃ ないからね。
English: I guess that makes sense... You've never left Kurain village, after all.

Japanese: わたくし、この里から 出たことがないものですから。
English: I'm really sorry, Mr. Nick. I just don't really know anyone outside the village.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 倉院の里
English: June 21 Kurain Village

Japanese: あっ！
English: Ah!

Japanese: ぎゃっ！
English: AAAAAAAAH!

Japanese: あ、ちょ、ちょっと 待ってくださいよ！　ナツミさん！
English: H-Hey! Wait! Please! Lotta!

Japanese: （‥‥屋敷の中に 逃げていってしまった‥‥）
English: (She ran off into the manor...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 倉院の里
English: June 21 Kurain Village

Japanese: （手がかりは集まってきた）
English: (Phew. I think I've found everything I can.)

Japanese: （でも‥‥それを どうつなげればいいのか？）
English: (But I can't see how they all add up... Hmm...)

Japanese: （それに、のどかさんがここに いるのは、グーゼンなのか？）
English: (And is Ini Miney being here just a coincidence...?)

Japanese: ‥‥なあ、ニイちゃん？
English: ...Hey, Mister...

Japanese: （呼び出された看護師の霊は、 霧崎先生を恨んでいた）
English: (How is it that the spirit Dr. Grey wanted to call just happened to be that nurse,)

Japanese: （その霊の妹が、たまたま ここにいた‥‥。不自然だ）
English: (and that nurse turns out to be Ini's sister... It's too... convenient...)

Japanese: ‥‥おい、ナルホドー！
English: ...Hey! Phoenix! I'm talkin' to you!

Japanese: （のどかさんについて、 何か情報がほしいんだけど‥‥）
English: (I wish I knew a little more about Ini, but where am I going to find info like that?)

Japanese: ああ、そうかいそうかい！
English: Oh, I get it.

Japanese: ウチみたいな人間のクズには、 シカトきめこむっちゅうワケやな！
English: Ya can't be bothered to look over at trash like me. Is that it, Mr. High-and-Mighty!?

Japanese: ‥‥うわっ！　な、ナツミさん！
English: Aah! L-Lotta!

Japanese: あっ！　気づいてくれはった！
English: Oh, so ya finally noticed!

Japanese: （耳もとで思いっきり叫んどいて ナニ言ってんだよ‥‥）
English: (I thought I heard a mosquito buzzing in my ear, although I couldn't understand a word...)

Japanese: さすがナルホドーやッ！
English: Well, I gotta hand it to ya. Ya sure know how to chase down garbage!

Japanese: こんな粗大ゴミのコト、ここまで 追いかけてきてくれはって！
English: I mean, I'm grateful to ya for chasin' this large lump of one around like this.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: Um... Thanks...?

Japanese: ‥‥でも、アンタ。 時間かかりすぎやで。
English: But ya know, you're just wastin' yer time.

Japanese: ウチ、ほんのちょっぴり さみしかったやん。
English: I'll be fine on my own.

Japanese: な、ナニ言ってんですか。
English: Wh-What are you going on about?

Japanese: “追うな”って言ったの、 ナツミさんじゃないですか！
English: And as for "following" or "chasing" or whatever it is, isn't that what YOU'RE doing!?

Japanese: まま、それはアレや。
English: Um, well, ya see...

Japanese: ビミョーにゆれ動くオトメ心、 いうヤツやな。
English: Well, yer not exactly a real gentleman with a lady's heart, are ya?

Japanese: （‥‥ホントに 反省してるのかこの人‥‥？）
English: (You're not exactly the world's number one charmer either, sister.)

Japanese: ホンマ、申しわけない！
English: Look, I'm really dang sorry about today!

Japanese: ウチ、いったん思いこむと、 もう止まらんタチなんや。
English: When I get all fired up about somethin', it's impossible to stop me.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん、すんごいショック、 受けてましたよ。
English: You hurt Maya really badly, Lotta.

Japanese: ううう‥‥
English: Um...

Japanese: ユルしてくれへんの？ ウチのコト‥‥
English: I don't suppose you could forgive a gal like me...?

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、いいですよ。 真宵ちゃんも助かったことだし。
English: Well, I guess. You did help Maya out too, after all.

Japanese: ホンマか。
English: For real?

Japanese: ホンマです。
English: Yeah, for real.

Japanese: ‥‥よかったわぁ。 これでまた‥‥
English: Ah, thank goodness... I'm still...

Japanese: “これでまた”‥‥なんです？
English: "I'm still..." what...?

Japanese: い、いやいや。いやいやいやいや。 なんでもないでえ。なんでも。
English: N-No, no, nothing, nothing at all. Just talkin' to myself, ya know, gibberish...

Japanese: ‥‥よし！　この大沢木　ナツミ、 アンタらのチカラになったる！
English: ...Now then! Lotta Hart, at your service!

Japanese: ウチが知ってる情報、 ゼーンブ教えたるわ！
English: I'll share with ya all the dirt I've dug up!

Japanese: あ、ありがとうございます。
English: Th-Thanks.

Japanese: ビックリしちゃアカンで。 しかも今なら‥‥なんとタダやッ！
English: Hey, don't be all surprised! Tell ya what, I'll even give ya all this info for free!

Japanese: （‥‥金をとられたほうが ビックリするけどな‥‥）
English: (Lotta not taking money? Now I KNOW this one's an alien clone...)

Japanese: ‥‥それはまた、話がベツです。
English: I'm not done...

Japanese: う。
English: Um...

Japanese: 信じていたナツミさんに ウラぎられたせいで、
English: And to think we trusted you, Lotta, and what do you do!? You stab us in the back!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん、すっかり フケこんじゃいましたよ。
English: You stressed us all out and even gave Maya a few strands of white hair!

Japanese: ううう‥‥。
English: Urngh...

Japanese: これから彼女にワラ人形と５寸クギ を差し入れに行くところです。
English: Now I'm going to have to pray she makes it, or she may come back as a ghost...

Japanese: ぎゃー。それだけはやめてぇ！
English: Ack! That's enough! Ya can stop right there!

Japanese: ウチの知ってる情報、 みんな吐くから！
English: Look, I'll tell ya everything I know! Everything! I'll spill it all!

Japanese: ‥‥情報？
English: Everything?

Japanese: せや、せや、せや！
English: Yeah, ya got it! Everything!

Japanese: しかも。ええか‥‥、 思いきって‥‥、タダにしたるワ！
English: But... Well, actually... Tell ya what, I'll even do it for free!

Japanese: （アタリマエだ！）
English: (You were expecting me to pay you after what you did to us!?)

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんが 犯人じゃないとしたら‥‥、
English: If Maya is not the real murderer, then...

Japanese: ナツミさん、 だれが犯人だと思います？
English: who do you think is, Lotta?

Japanese: そんなん、決まってるやん。 あのオカルトマニアや。
English: Well heck, ain't it obvious? It's that occult freak!

Japanese: 葉中　のどか、ですか‥‥。
English: You mean Ini Miney?

Japanese: だって、他にいないやん。
English: Well, there ain't anyone else it could be, right?

Japanese: オバハンとウチとアンタは、 ずっといっしょにいたんやし。
English: That granny, you and me, we were all together that whole time...

Japanese: （ナツミさん、のどかさんのこと、 疑ってるのか‥‥）
English: (So, Lotta suspects Ini...)

Japanese: （もしかしたら、彼女の情報を まだニギっているかも‥‥）
English: (Which means she might have already found some info about our mysterious Ini...)

Japanese: 葉中　のどかについて、 もっと情報はありませんか？
English: Speaking of Ini, do you have any other information about her?

Japanese: あるで。
English: Sure.

Japanese: お‥‥教えてください！
English: Well, what is it!?

Japanese: ふっふっふっ‥‥ 情報は、高くつくでぇ‥‥！
English: Ha ha ha... It'll cost ya... And it'll cost a lot!

Japanese: （さっき、タダだって 言ってたじゃないか！）
English: (Oh, silly me. Here I thought "free" meant "no money necessary".)

Japanese: 彼女なぁ‥‥、 半年前まで、入院してたんや。
English: That girl was hospitalized about half a year ago.

Japanese: 入院‥‥。 どうしてですか？
English: Hospitalized...? But why?

Japanese: ‥‥さあな。ここから先は、 自分で調べるんや。
English: Well, heck, you can go find out on yer own.

Japanese: は？
English: Huh?

Japanese: はいこれ、病院のアドレス。 ナニかわかるかもしれんで。
English: Here, I'll give ya the clinic's address. Maybe ya can find out something.

Japanese: ナニかわかる“カモ”‥‥？
English: "Maybe"...? What's that supposed to mean!?

Japanese: ま、まあな。
English: Well...

Japanese: じつはウチ、メンドくさくて 病院までは行ってないんや。
English: See, actually, it was gonna be annoying, I reckoned, so I didn't actually go there.

Japanese: うう‥‥。
English: Aargh...

Japanese: ま。世の中なんて だいたい、こんなもんやで。
English: Well, that's the way the gumbo spoils, right?

Japanese: 世の中のせいに しないでくださいよ‥‥。
English: Spoiled gumbo sounds... Eww... I mean, look, don't blame this on... Oh nevermind!

Japanese: （しかたない。行ってみるか‥‥ この《堀田クリニック》へ）
English: (Looks like I don't have much of a choice. Better go check out this "Hotti Clinic"...)

Japanese: この新聞記事なんですけど‥‥
English: About this clipping...

Japanese: ええかぁ、ナルホドー。
English: Now, you listen here, boy, and listen good.

Japanese: そんな古新聞に こだわってちゃアカン。
English: You can't go lookin' at such an old piece of paper, ya hear!?

Japanese: 人間の目ェが、なんで 前についとるか知ってるか？
English: People's got eyes on the front side. You know why?

Japanese: 前を見るためやッ！
English: So they can see in front of themselves! Not behind!

Japanese: ‥‥どや。 グッと来たやろ？　な？
English: So how about it? You feelin' my energy?

Japanese: （来るワケない）
English: (Nope, not an electron.)

Japanese: いやー、ウチって、よっぽど コイツにインネンがあるなぁ。
English: Ya think I could have the guts to carry one of these around?

Japanese: ピストルの似合う、マショーの 芸能カメラマン、かぁ。
English: A celebrity photographer with a gun? Ya think it'd work? "Bang, bang!!"

Japanese: ‥‥なんや。 イメージ、ぐちゃぐちゃやな。
English: ...Nah... Thanks, but nah. I can't see myself like that.

Japanese: ぎゃー。 血ィついとるで、血ィ！
English: Aaaaah! Th-There's... There's blood on it!!

Japanese: 見せんな！　こんなモン！
English: Get that away from me! Where do you get off showin' somethin' like this to me!?

Japanese: （‥‥情報のカケラも 手に入らないな、このヒト‥‥）
English: (Oops, guess that was the wrong thing to show her...)

Japanese: さっすが、ウチやな。
English: I'm a real natural, aren't I?

Japanese: 事件をバッチリとらえてる上に、 ちょっぴりミステリアス。
English: I caught the murder just right, and even managed to make it mysterious.

Japanese: ミステリアス‥‥って、 ピントがボケてるだけ‥‥
English: Mysterious!? All you managed to do was forget to focus your lens!

Japanese: やかましい！　それが ウチのテクニックやッ！
English: Shaddup! That's my technique! My technique, now, ya hear!?

Japanese: ‥‥知らん。 なーんも知らん。
English: Nope. Don't know nothin' about that.

Japanese: ウチ、キョーミのないモンには 目もくれないんや。
English: Things that don't catch my eye, don't catch my interest.

Japanese: （イヤなジャーナリスト‥‥）
English: (And she calls herself a journalist??)

Japanese: たしか、この前の事件でも 見かけたカオやな。
English: I swear I've seen his face before. Maybe during that last case...

Japanese: 事件が起こると かならずおるよな、この刑事。
English: Whenever something happens, this detective guy's always there.

Japanese: なんや、ホンマは犯人ちゃうんか？ コイツ。
English: I wonder if he's the real baddie behind everything?

Japanese: （‥‥そのうち、ぼくまで 疑われそうだな‥‥）
English: (Somehow, I highly doubt it...)

Japanese: お。霧崎センセやな。 キノドクなこっちゃなあ。
English: Hey, it's that Dr. Grey guy. It's a pity what happened to him.

Japanese: やっぱり、オバケはコワいわ。 くわばらくわばら。
English: Pretty scary what ghouls and ghosts can do to a man, huh? Gawd rest his poor soul.

Japanese: （うーん。ナツミさん、 コシが引けてるなあ‥‥）
English: (Hmm... Since when was Lotta so religious...?)

Japanese: うーん‥‥ こいつは見落としとったワ。
English: Hmm... I overlooked this little kid.

Japanese: な、何がですか‥‥？
English: Wh-What do you mean?

Japanese: ウチ、思うんやけど‥‥ この子ちゃうか？　ハンニン。
English: I mean, I didn't think it, but ya know how it's always 'em silent types?

Japanese: な、なな、なんでですか！
English: H-Huh!? Wait, wait, wait...

Japanese: だって‥‥イチバン アヤシくなさそうやろ？
English: The little tyke's nothin' but a ball of happy goodness, right?

Japanese: だいたいそういうヤツが ハンニンや。
English: So I reckon, she's the type to have done it!

Japanese: ま。世の中なんて そんなもんやで。
English: Well, now, that's just how the world turns.

Japanese: 世の中を持ち出さないで くださいよ‥‥。
English: I don't think this has anything to do with how the world turns...

Japanese: しっかし、この子も ツイてないよなあ。
English: Now, this kid -- I think she needs to be taught some manners.

Japanese: たしか、２回目やろ？ ロウヤにぶちこまれるの。
English: This here's her second time in the stew, right? My Pa woulda slapped me straight!

Japanese: そ、そうですね。
English: Y-Yeah...

Japanese: 気ぃつけるんやで。 ２度あることは３度‥‥
English: Ya know what they say, if there's a second time, there's bound to be a third, and a fo-

Japanese: ぎゃっ！
English: OW!

Japanese: ‥‥うう。ナルホドーが ぶったぁぁ‥‥。
English: Nngh... Ya didn't have to slap me. I get it, I get it. Sorry...

Japanese: ‥‥なんや、そろそろ あきてきたな、このヘアスタイル。
English: Ya know, I'm beginnin' to get sick of this hairstyle.

Japanese: 今度は、ストレートパーマ かけちゃろかなァ。
English: Next time I go to the salon, I'm gonna get my hair straightened, I reckon.

Japanese: なぁ、ナルホドー、 どう思う？
English: What do ya think, Phoenix?

Japanese: ‥‥たぶん、だれだか わからなくなると思いますよ。
English: I think if you got rid of your afro, I wouldn't recognize who you were.

Japanese: それよなー。モンダイは。
English: Yeah, that's kinda the problem, ain't it?

Japanese: （たのむから、事件のコト 話してくれぇ‥‥）
English: (Please, can we get on with the important stuff...? Say, like the murderer...)

Japanese: あのオバハン、 ハクリョクあったなあ。
English: That old lady sure can be forceful.

Japanese: ちょっぴりビビったワ。 ‥‥ま、ほんのちょっぴりだけど。
English: She scared me that day. ...Well, only a little, now, ya hear?

Japanese: こいつ、検事やな。 ‥‥あれはルール違反やで。
English: Hey, this is that prosecutor gal. The one that likes breakin' all the rules.

Japanese: ムチはアカン。 ムチャアカン。
English: Switches are for horses and lights, not people!

Japanese: ‥‥どうや？　今の！ ちょっとシャレとるやろ？
English: How's that? I made a pretty clever pun, didn't I?

Japanese: ‥‥聞いてませんでした。
English: ...Sorry, didn't hear you.

Japanese: なんや、アマいなー。 何度でも聞かしたるで。
English: Ah, well, I'll forgive the wax in yer ears this once and tell it to ya one more time.

Japanese: 葉中　のどか‥‥か。
English: Heh, that's Ini Miney, right?

Japanese: 何か、ご存じないですか？ ‥‥彼女のコト。
English: Yeah. I was wondering if you know anything about this girl...

Japanese: せやなあ。じつは‥‥ ちょっと調べてはみたんや。
English: Ya know! Actually, I did a little checkin' up on her the other day.

Japanese: 彼女、妹なんやで。 ‥‥霧崎センセが呼び出した霊の。
English: Her sister, she's the one that Dr. Grey wanted to call back from the spirit world.

Japanese: ええ。それは聞いてます。
English: Yeah, I had heard as much...

Japanese: のどかさんが紹介したんですよ。 先生に、この倉院の里のことを。
English: I had also heard that Ini was the one to introduce Dr. Grey to Kurain Village.

Japanese: そうやったんか‥‥。 アヤしいな、そりゃ。
English: Ya don't say? That's one sneaky gal, if you ask me...

Japanese: ‥‥なんやったら、 もうチョイくわしく話そかぁ？
English: Hey, ya want me to tell ya more about her?

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 倉院の里
English: June 21 Kurain Village

Japanese: あ！　どやった！ 行ってみたんか、病院は？
English: Hey! So how was it!? Didja go to the clinic?

Japanese: ええ。いろいろ聞いて きたんですけど‥‥。
English: Yeah... I found out a few things... I guess...

Japanese: ‥‥なんや。 ハギレ悪いなぁ。
English: ...What's the matter? Ya ain't lookin' so well.

Japanese: あ。そういえばナ。さっき、 オバハンがケーサツ呼んだで。
English: Ah, yeah. So earlier, that ol' granny called the cops.

Japanese: ‥‥え。 キミ子さんが‥‥警察を？
English: ...Huh? The cops? Why would Morgan call the police?

Japanese: “話がある”ゆうてな。 なんや、オモロなってきたなァ。
English: She said she had "somethin' to tell 'em". Things are sure gettin' interestin'.

Japanese: それにしても、あのオバハン ホンマついてないなー。
English: Ah, but honestly, I tell ya, that granny sure got her bloomers in a knot.

Japanese: まあ、そうですね。
English: Well, I guess you could put it that way...

Japanese: たった１つの小石に けつまづいたおかげで、
English: It always starts out small, ya know?

Japanese: 家元の座はうばわれるは、 里の人間からはバカにされるは‥‥
English: Stealin' the Master's seat, and controllin' the village folk...

Japanese: あげくの果てに、こないな 殺人事件まで起こってしもうて。
English: and now ya see what it's turned into? A murder, that's what.

Japanese: まったくですね。
English: Yeah, what a mess.

Japanese: って、ちょっと待ってください！
English: H-Hey, hold on!

Japanese: なんや。
English: What?

Japanese: 今のハナシ‥‥はじめて聞くコト ばっかりだったんですけど。
English: That stuff you were just talking about... That's the first I've heard of it.

Japanese: えー、ホンマかァ？
English: Heh, really?

Japanese: この世界じゃァ、ダレでも 知ってるコトやで。
English: Well, it's old news to everyone else in the world.

Japanese: （なぜだ‥‥？ だれも教えてくれなかったぞ！）
English: (Well, excuse me for not keeping up with gossip while I'm busy saving people!)

Japanese: たしか、“家元の座をうばわれた” ‥‥って言いましたよね‥‥？
English: You said something about "stealing the Master's seat"?

Japanese: そうや。
English: Yup.

Japanese: 今、家元は綾里　舞子っちゅう 霊媒師なんやけど‥‥
English: Right now, the Master is this medium named Misty Fey, but...

Japanese: ホンマは、あのオバハンが 家元になるはずやったんや。
English: the one that was really supposed to be the Master was that old granny.

Japanese: （キミ子さんが‥‥家元‥‥？）
English: (Morgan was supposed to become... the Master...?)

Japanese: いやナ。なんでも、家元は 長女がつぐものらしいんや。
English: So I hear the Master has always been the oldest daughter.

Japanese: 長女‥‥ってことは‥‥
English: The oldest daughter...?

Japanese: キミ子はな。姉なんや。 綾里　舞子の‥‥
English: Turns out, that old bat is Misty Fey's older sister...

Japanese: そ、そうだったんですか。
English: O-Oh?

Japanese: フツーはな。姉のほうが 強いらしいで。‥‥霊力。
English: They say the older sister is usually the one with more spiritual power.

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥ムカシ、 真宵ちゃんも言ってたな）
English: (Come to think of it, Maya said the same thing a long time ago...)

Japanese: （お姉ちゃん‥‥千尋さんの霊力は シャレにならなかった、って）
English: (She warned me not to take Mia's powers lightly...)

Japanese: ‥‥ところが。
English: Well, anyway...

Japanese: どうしたワケか、あのオバハン、 妹の舞子に負けちまったワケや。
English: somehow, granny there lost to her younger sister, Misty.

Japanese: そうだったんですか‥‥
English: I see...

Japanese: 里の連中から、それはそれは ケーベツされたらしいで。
English: This made the townsfolk turn against her, so I hear. Said all sorta things too, like,

Japanese: “長女のクセに、家元を つげなかった”ってナ。
English: "She's the oldest, and yet she doesn't have the power to be the next Master. Disgraceful."

Japanese: （そんなことがあったのか‥‥）
English: (Wow. I had no idea...)

Japanese: ‥‥もし、キミ子さんの霊力が 強かったら‥‥？
English: ...So what would've happened had Morgan been stronger...?

Japanese: 真宵はんは、分家のムスメ、 ってコトになってたやろな。
English: Maya's family woulda been the branch family, I reckon.

Japanese: のどかさんは‥‥？
English: Do you know where Ini is, Lotta?

Japanese: ああ。さっき見かけたで。 ボンヤリ庭、見とったワ。
English: Yeah, I saw her not too long ago. She's hangin' around that garden in the middle.

Japanese: そうですか‥‥。
English: OK, thanks.

Japanese: なんや。話でもあるんか？
English: Ya got somethin' to ask her?

Japanese: （彼女には、いろいろ 聞くべきことがあるぞ‥‥）
English: (Yeah. I've got a whole list of things only she can clear up for me...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 倉院の里
English: June 21 Kurain Village

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥？ あそこで、なんかモメてるぞ‥‥。
English: Looks like something's up...

Japanese: お‥‥おかあさまを！ おかあさまをイジめないで！
English: S-Stop... Stop! Leave my mother alone!

Japanese: い‥‥いたたッ。まま、待つッス！ そんなつもりは‥‥
English: O-Ow! H-Hey, hold on, OK, pal!? I'm not gonna hurt...

Japanese: 春美ちゃん。 だいじょうぶでござあますよ。
English: Pearl, dear. It is perfectly alright.

Japanese: ほんの少し、この殿方と お出かけするだけでござあます。
English: I will be out for a short while with this young man.

Japanese: おかあさま！　わたくしも ごいっしょに‥‥！
English: Mother! Please, let me go too!

Japanese: ‥‥ダメです。 あなたは、屋敷をお守りなさい。
English: No, you must stay here and protect the manor.

Japanese: うう‥‥
English: But...

Japanese: そ、そんなカオしちゃダメッス。 あっ！　そッス！
English: Aww, don't make that face. Hey, I'll tell you what!

Japanese: 今、イイもの見せてあげるッス。 ホンモノのピストルを‥‥
English: I'll show you something cool. Here, look! It's a real pistol...

Japanese: ‥‥刑事さん！ まいりますわよ。
English: Detective! Do not upset me further!

Japanese: うう‥‥。 こりゃどうも、すまねッス。
English: Ah... Sorry...

Japanese: （‥‥キミ子さん、イトノコ刑事を つれて行っちまったぞ‥‥）
English: (...Poor Gumshoe... Being dragged off by the ear like that by Morgan... Owww...)

Japanese: おかあさま‥‥。
English: Mother...

Japanese: おかあさま‥‥
English: Mother...

Japanese: 今までこんなこと、 一度もなかったのに‥‥。
English: This has never happened before...

Japanese: 春美ちゃん‥‥
English: Pearls...

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Mr. Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥わたくし、とてもイヤな 予感がします。
English: ...I have a really bad feeling that I can't shake.

Japanese: 何か、とても悪いことが 起こるような‥‥
English: I think something really bad is going to happen...

Japanese: こわいです。 ‥‥なるほどくん‥‥
English: I'm scared... ...I'm really scared, Mr. Nick...

Japanese: （弱ったな‥‥ かけるコトバも見あたらないぞ）
English: (Say something, Wright! Anything...! *sigh* I'm terrible at this.)

Japanese: なるほどくん！ 真宵さま‥‥真宵さまは？
English: Mr. Nick, what about... What about Mystic Maya?

Japanese: だいじょうぶだよ。 ‥‥材料はそろったと思う。
English: She'll be alright. I think I've gathered enough evidence...

Japanese: きっと‥‥きっと、真宵さまも ココロ細いと思います。
English: Mystic Maya... She must feel all alone.

Japanese: なるほどくん！ 行ってあげてください！
English: Please, Mr. Nick! Go visit her!

Japanese: え。でも‥‥春美ちゃんを 置いては行けないよ。
English: Yeah, but... I can't just leave you here all alone, either.

Japanese: わ、わたくしなら だいじょうぶです！
English: I-I'll be fine!

Japanese: おねがいです！　真宵さまの そばにいてあげてください！
English: Please! Mystic Maya needs you by her side!

Japanese: （春美ちゃんのまなざしが つきささってくる‥‥）
English: (Her stare is really piercing... *shiver*)

Japanese: 春美ちゃん‥‥あの、 コレなんだけど‥‥。
English: Hey, Pearls... Could you take a look at...

Japanese: ‥‥ぐすっ‥‥
English: ...*sniffle*...

Japanese: （かわいそうに。 ココロ細いんだな‥‥）
English: (Aww... I can't do this to her now. She looks so pitiful and sad...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・わたりろうか
English: June 21 Fey Manor Winding Way

Japanese: （人の気配はないな‥‥）
English: (Looks like there's no one here...)

Japanese: （今日もいい天気だ。 ココロの中はどしゃ降りだけど）
English: (And even though the weather is gorgeous today, it's raining inside my heart...)

Japanese: これまたずいぶんと 古いツボだな。
English: It's a really old urn.

Japanese: そこら中に ヒビが入っているぞ。
English: There are the ashes of a person long dead inside.

Japanese: 何か、いわれのある ものなんだろう‥‥。
English: It looks like something with a long history...

Japanese: キョーミがないので、 とりあえずほっとくか。
English: Other than that, it's not very interesting, so time to move on.

Japanese: あそこはたしか 《控えの間》だ。
English: That's the "Side Room".

Japanese: お客さんが休む部屋 のようだ。
English: Looks like guests can use it as a place to take a break when they feel tired.

Japanese: 小型の焼却炉だ。
English: It's a small incinerator.

Japanese: この里には、ゴミの収集車は まわってこないのだろう。
English: I don't think garbage trucks come all the way out here to a little village like this.

Japanese: ゴミは、それぞれの家で 処理しているようだ。
English: So every family has to burn their own trash.

Japanese: 灯ろうに、ししおどし。 リッパな庭だなあ。
English: A splendid garden with a small lamp and traditional decorations.

Japanese: ‥‥あそぶには ちょっとせまいけど。
English: It's a bit small to walk around in...

Japanese: でも、あの焼却炉が 気になるなあ。
English: Although, that incinerator piques my curiosity...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・わたりろうか
English: June 21 Fey Manor Winding Way

Japanese: （おや。焼却炉のところに だれかいるな‥‥）
English: (Hey, there's someone by the incinerator...)

Japanese: （あ。‥‥こっちに 気がついたみたいだ）
English: (Ooh, looks like they've noticed me.)

Japanese: （ものすごいイキオイで 走ってくるぞ‥‥）
English: (And are now running at top speed towards me...)

Japanese: あらあ！　はあ‥‥はあ‥‥
English: Oh, hey! *huff, huff*

Japanese: はあ‥‥はあ‥‥ たしかあなたは、歯医者の。
English: Like, *huff, huff* You're that dentist guy.

Japanese: 弁護士の成歩堂　龍一です。 どうぞよろしく。
English: Actually, it's Phoenix Wright, attorney at law. Nice to see you again.

Japanese: まあ。ごていねいに。 こちらこそ、よろしくう。
English: Ooh, like, real suave. So, it's, like, my turn. It's nice to see you, too.

Japanese: どうしたんですか？ イキを切らせて‥‥
English: So, what's up? You're all out of breath...

Japanese: え、ええ？ ‥‥そおですねえ‥‥
English: L-Like, huh? Oh! Like you mean...

Japanese: 私、ちょっとコーフン しちゃってるんですよお。
English: Like, I'm just totally happy and like, totally excited to be here.

Japanese: コーフン？
English: Excited?

Japanese: だってここ、霊媒の本場だから。 見るべきモノが多くて。
English: Like, I mean, there are so many things to, like, see! It's, like, the real deal here!

Japanese: へえ‥‥。たとえば、 何があるんですか？
English: Oh, really? For example...?

Japanese: たとえば‥‥あのツボとか。 ご存じですかあ？
English: For example... Like, that urn over there. Do you, like, know about it?

Japanese: （あそこにかざってあるヤツか。 古ぼけたツボにしか見えないぞ）
English: (All I see is an old, cracked piece of pottery...)

Japanese: ‥‥こいつにタマシイがねえ‥‥。 見れば見るほど、ヒビだらけだ。
English: So there's a spirit in this urn, huh? Hmm, all I see are a bunch of cracks.

Japanese: スキ間だらけで、タマシイなんか もれちゃいそうだけどな。
English: But I guess maybe the spirit could live in the space between the cracks...

Japanese: なんだ、字が書いてあるぞ‥‥ “子供”‥‥コドモ？　ヘンなの。
English: Hmm, looks like something's written on it... "I AM"? Huh? Weird...

Japanese: 証拠品《倉院のツボ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Sacred Urn added to the Court Record.

Japanese: そういえば、春美ちゃんが 言ってたっけ。
English: That's right...

Japanese: 焼却炉の中で、 このカギを見つけた、って
English: Pearls said she found this key inside the incinerator.

Japanese: ちゃんと調べてみるか。 ‥‥焼却炉の中‥‥
English: I guess I should take a look inside for myself...

Japanese: こ、これは！　どう見ても、 ‥‥ま、真宵ちゃんの‥‥！
English: Th-This... This is from Maya's costume...!

Japanese: 血‥‥がついてるな。 ほんの少しだけど。
English: There's blood on it... A tiny bit, but still...

Japanese: 証拠品《布きれ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Cloth Scrap added to the Court Record.

Japanese: この焼却炉で、 こいつが燃やされていた。
English: So, this piece of cloth was burned in this incinerator.

Japanese: ここからは、このカギも 見つかっている。
English: And this key was also found in there.

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、今回の事件を 解決するポイントになりそうだな。
English: Hmm, I get the feeling this incinerator plays a crucial part in this murder...

Japanese: あのツボ‥‥。 言い伝えでもあるんですか？
English: Is there some sort of legend connected with that urn?

Japanese: あれってえ。 この里の宝物なんですよお。
English: Like, yeah! It's, like, this village's treasure!

Japanese: へえ。
English: Huh. Didn't know that.

Japanese: あの中にですねえ。 封じこめられてるんですってえ。
English: And, like, it's sealed inside that urn.

Japanese: ‥‥あの。ナニが‥‥？
English: ...Um... "It's"...? What are you talking about?

Japanese: 決まってるじゃないですかあ。
English: Like, you know!

Japanese: アヤサト　キョウコの タマシイですよお。
English: Ami Fey's spirit!

Japanese: アヤサト‥‥キョウコ‥‥ だれですか、それ？
English: Ami... Fey...? Who's that?

Japanese: いやだなあ、田中さん。
English: Like, jeez, Mr. Smith. Get with the program!

Japanese: ホラ。倉院流霊媒道の創始者の キョウコさまですよお。
English: Everyone knows the lady that founded the Kurain Channeling Technique was Mystic Ami.

Japanese: “ホラ”と言われましても‥‥。
English: Um, you don't have to get upset with me...

Japanese: あのツボにタマシイが 宿っているかぎり、
English: So, like, as long as her spirit, like, lives in that urn,

Japanese: 綾里家の霊力は不滅なんですよお。 ‥‥ロマンチックですよねえ‥‥
English: the spiritual power of the Fey family'll stick around. Like, isn't that totally romantic?

Japanese: ‥‥ろまんちっく‥‥
English: Umm... Romantic, huh...?

Japanese: （そんなありがたいツボなら、 何か手がかりになるかもな‥‥）
English: (If that urn is really as important as she says, it could be a vital clue...)

Japanese: のどかさん。 事件のことですけど。
English: Ini, I want to ask you about the murder...

Japanese: じけん‥‥？
English: Murder...?

Japanese: ホラ。霧崎先生の。 （他に何があるんだよ‥‥）
English: Dr. Grey's... Remember...? (As if there's another one?)

Japanese: ああ‥‥。ちゃんと、 そう言っていただかないとお。
English: Oh, that one! Like, you shoulda said so in the first place!

Japanese: 私、なんにも知りませえん。 “控えの間”で寝てましたから。
English: So, like, I totally don't know anything 'cause I was, like, sleeping in the Side Room.

Japanese: ‥‥手がかりがないんです。 気づいたこととか、ありませんか？
English: Well, that's not much help... Did you notice anything at all?

Japanese: そう言われても‥‥ 興味ないんですう。
English: Like, come on. What a total bore.

Japanese: 私には、なんの関係もない 事件だしい‥‥。
English: I, like, totally have nothing to do with this, like, murder thing...

Japanese: （霧崎先生に倉院の里を紹介 したのは、のどかさんだ）
English: (Oh, yeah. You only introduced Dr. Grey to Kurain Village. No relation, sure.)

Japanese: （つまり“知り合い”だったのに ‥‥“関係ない”はないだろう）
English: (Even if you were only "acquaintances", that still means you knew him.)

Japanese: （交通事故で亡くなったお姉さんは 霧崎外科の看護師だったんだし）
English: (Furthermore, your sister, who died in that accident, was a nurse at his clinic...)

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥お姉さんの 交通事故について教えてください。
English: Alright then. Why don't you tell me about your sister's fatal accident.

Japanese: どうして？　今回の事件と 関係があるんですかあ？
English: Like, why? You think, like, it's got something to, like, do with the murder?

Japanese: それは、わかりません。 でもとにかく、何か手がかりが‥‥
English: I don't know yet, but I'm trying to chase down every lead I have...

Japanese: （‥‥これは‥‥ サイコ・ロック‥‥！）
English: (...A... A Psyche-Lock!)

Japanese: ‥‥どうかしましたかあ？
English: Like, what is it?

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥クチを開かせる 必要があるな‥‥）
English: (Looks like I'm going to need something to pry her mouth open on this one...)

Japanese: あの、のどかさん。 事故のことなんですけど‥‥
English: Um, about the accident, Ini...

Japanese: ‥‥あ。その目。 まだ、あきらめてませんねえ？
English: Like, you're totally not gonna give up, are you?

Japanese: はい。
English: No, I'm not.

Japanese: お姉さんのこと、 もう忘れたいんですよお。
English: I totally want to forget about my sister, you know? Time to move on and all that.

Japanese: （‥‥やっぱり、ロックを 解除するしかないな‥‥）
English: (Guess I have no choice but to remove those locks...)

Japanese: そう‥‥。 あの日、私は‥‥乗っていました。
English: Yeah... I was... riding...

Japanese: あのクルマに‥‥ お姉さんといっしょに。
English: Riding in that car... with my sister.

Japanese: うちに帰る途中だったんです。
English: We were... like, on our way home...

Japanese: 私、助手席に乗っていました。 でも、眠りこんじゃって‥‥。
English: I was riding in the passenger seat when I fell asleep...

Japanese: ‥‥あとは、記事のとおりです。
English: ...and then, it happened.

Japanese: とっさにハンドバッグだけ持って、 助手席のドアから逃げた‥‥
English: At the time, I grabbed for my purse, and escaped from the passenger-side door.

Japanese: ‥‥お姉さんは？
English: ...And your sister...?

Japanese: どうしようもなかったの。
English: I, like, couldn't do anything...

Japanese: 車の中はケムリでいっぱいで‥‥ 何も見えなかった。
English: The car was full of smoke... I couldn't see anything...

Japanese: そうだったんですか‥‥
English: Oh, I'm sorry...

Japanese: あの事故は、霧崎先生が睡眠薬を 盛ったせいで起こったわけですか。
English: Did Dr. Grey really cause that accident by drugging your sister with sleeping pills?

Japanese: もしかして、疑ってるんですか？ ‥‥私のこと。
English: Like, he was totally out to get my sister...

Japanese: お姉さんのカタキをうつために、 霧崎先生を‥‥、って。
English: He totally did that to, like, pay my sister back...

Japanese: （そりゃそうだろう‥‥）
English: (Hmm, I wonder...)

Japanese: （霧崎先生を殺害する動機は、 今のところ、これしかない！）
English: (This does give Ini a good reason to want to murder Dr. Grey!)

Japanese: ‥‥ふふふ。 タンジュンなんですねえ。
English: Ha ha ha. It seems simple, doesn't it?

Japanese: どういうことですか？
English: What do you mean!?

Japanese: ‥‥ムリだと思いますよお。 私を追いつめるのは。
English: But, it would be very hard to catch me. Actually, I'd say impossible.

Japanese: なんですって‥‥
English: What!?

Japanese: ‥‥今にわかりますよお。
English: I think you get the picture.

Japanese: たたきのめしてあげちゃいます！ ‥‥楽しみですねえ。
English: I'm going to serve you a slice of humble pie! I hope you enjoy it.

Japanese: ふふふ‥‥。
English: Ha ha ha...

Japanese: ～交通事故～
English: -- Traffic Accident --

Japanese: 聞かせてもらいましょう。 ‥‥交通事故について。
English: Please tell me about the traffic accident.

Japanese: え？　だれの事故ですかあ？
English: Huh? Like, whose accident?

Japanese: き、決まってるでしょう。 お姉さんの事故ですよ！
English: Your sister's accident, naturally!

Japanese: あ、ああ‥‥そうですよねえ。
English: Oh... Um, Oh, yeah...

Japanese: お姉さんは眠らされたんですう。 あの霧崎先生に‥‥。
English: So, like, that Dr. Grey guy made my sister fall asleep...

Japanese: それ以上、お話しするコトなんて ありませえん。
English: And, like, that's all I have to say about that.

Japanese: ううん‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: （‥‥今、のどかさん、 気になることを言ったな‥‥）
English: (Interesting...)

Japanese: （“だれの事故ですかあ？” ‥‥って）
English: (Ini just asked, "Whose accident?"...)

Japanese: （それって、つまり‥‥）
English: (Which means...)

Japanese: （未実さん以外の“交通事故” の情報を知ってる、ってコトだ）
English: (She must know something about another person's accident...)

Japanese: じゃあ、のどかさん。 聞かせてもらえますか。
English: Alright, let's change the topic, then.

Japanese: ‥‥別の人物の“交通事故” について‥‥。
English: How about you tell me about a certain other person's accident?

Japanese: 別のヒトの‥‥交通事故。 いったい、だれの事故ですかあ？
English: Huh? Another person's accident...? Like, you got someone in mind?

Japanese: ‥‥へええ。その人も 交通事故にあったんですか？
English: Oh wow. So, like, that person had a car accident too?

Japanese: 知らないとは言わせませんよ。
English: You can't tell me you don't know.

Japanese: でも、知ってるとも 言えませんけどお。
English: Like, how would I know if that person, like, had an accident?

Japanese: え。
English: Oh.

Japanese: 私、オカルトにはくわしいですけど 交通事故マニアじゃないですしい。
English: Besides, like, I'm an occult fan, not an ambulance chaser like you.

Japanese: わかりました。じゃ、また 他の人のことを聞きます！
English: Alright, I get it. OK, then let's see if you know about this person's accident!

Japanese: のどかさん‥‥ あなた自身の交通事故ですよ。
English: I want to ask you about your own car accident.

Japanese: わ‥‥私‥‥ですか！ M...
English: Me!?

Japanese: そうです。
English: Yes.

Japanese: おかしいですねえ。 知りませんよお、事故なんて。
English: So, wow, like, how strange. I have, like, no idea what you're talking about.

Japanese: 証拠でもあるんですかあ？ 私が交通事故にあった、っていう。
English: Can you, like, prove I was in, like, an accident?

Japanese: これが、その証拠です。
English: Here is your proof.

Japanese: ‥‥あのお。なんだか私 悲しくなってきましたあ。
English: ...Like, this is so totally bringing me down.

Japanese: ど、どういうことですか？
English: H-Huh?

Japanese: 私がノンビリ屋さんだからって、 バカにしてませんかあ？
English: I know I'm, like, a little flakey, but do you really, like, think I'm an idiot?

Japanese: い、いえ。そんなことは‥‥。
English: Ah, um, no... I didn't mean to suggest that...

Japanese: 私が交通事故にあった、っていう 証拠ですよお、念のため。
English: Like, let's make sure we're on the same page. I, like, want proof I was in an accident.

Japanese: （‥‥やんわりとたしなめられた。 しかし‥‥）
English: (I thought I'd ease into this topic, but...)

Japanese: なんと言われても、あなたは 交通事故にあってるはずです！
English: It doesn't matter what you say, I know you were in a car accident!

Japanese: これを見てください。 １年前の新聞記事です。
English: Would you take a look at this? It's a newspaper article from one year ago.

Japanese: 《生存者・葉中　のどかさんの話》 ‥‥って書いてあります。
English: It says, "Ms. Ini Miney Holds Interview About Crash".

Japanese: ‥‥あっ！
大ケガして、 入院したんですよね？
English: You suffered a very bad injury and had to be admitted to a hospital, correct?

Japanese: ちょ‥‥ちょっと、 待ってくださあい。
English: L-Like, wait!

Japanese: なんですか？
English: What now?

Japanese: それ、私じゃありませんよお。
English: Th-That's not me!

Japanese: なんですって！
English: Huh!?

Japanese: きっと、たまたま同じ名前の 別のヒトですよお。
English: I mean, like, this person has, like, the same name, but she's totally not me.

Japanese: ほら。“葉中　のどか”なんて、 ありふれた名前ですからあ。
English: My name, "Ini Miney" is, like, pretty common.

Japanese: ナニ言ってるんですか！
English: What in the world are you babbling about!?

Japanese: お姉さんの名前が“葉中　未実” って、書いてあるでしょう！
English: Your sister's name, "Mimi Miney" is in here too!

Japanese: いやあ‥‥コワいですよねー。 グーゼン、って、ホント。
English: Like, totally, wow... What a scary coincidence! I'm totally getting, like, goosebumps.

Japanese: （そ‥‥そんなイイワケが とおるのか！）
English: (Y-You think I'm going to let you get away with that!?)

Japanese: それとも、なんですかあ？
English: So, like, now what?

Japanese: 証拠でもあるんですかあ？ この私が入院していた、っていう。
English: Like, do you have something that totally says I was hospitalized?

Japanese: あの。ナニゴトも投げちゃダメだと 思うんです。スプーンを。
English: Um, like, you shouldn't throw, like, truffling things around, you know?

Japanese: ‥‥スプーン？ （ああ、“サジを投げる”ね‥‥）
English: ...Truffling? (Um, "trifling", maybe?)

Japanese: もっと、 アタマ使いましょうよお。
English: So, like, let's use our brains, OK?

Japanese: これが、その証拠です。
English: This is your proof.

Japanese: ‥‥そ、その写真は‥‥？
English: ...Like, what's that picture supposed to mean?

Japanese: あなたが入院していた病院で もらってきたんです。
English: I got this from the hospital where you were hospitalized at.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうコト？
English: H-Huh? So?

Japanese: 患者さんにね。 あなたのファンがいたんですよ。
English: There was a certain patient there. He's quite a fan...

Japanese: 記念に、とっておいたそうです。 ‥‥どう見ても、あなたです。
English: As a memento of your stay, he stole this. I have to say, this photo looks like you.

Japanese: ‥‥あのニセ院長！
English: ...That creepy fake Director!

Japanese: さあ。今度こそ聞かせてください。 事故のこと‥‥
English: Now are you ready to tell me about the accident...?

Japanese: ちょっと待ってくださあい。
English: Like, wait a sec.

Japanese: たしかに私、 事故にあいましたあ。
English: OK, so, like, I was in an accident. Sure.

Japanese: でも、それがなんですかあ？ 今度の事件と関係ないでしょお？
English: But that's, like, totally not related to this murder!

Japanese: 関係はあるはずです。
English: Actually, it is.

Japanese: 新聞記事を見てください。
English: Look at this article.

Japanese: 《女性１人が死亡した》 って書いてありますね。
English: It says that "one of the women died", correct?

Japanese: ‥‥そ‥‥それが？
English: ...A-And?

Japanese: この、“死亡した女性” というのは、だれだったんですか？
English: That woman who died -- who do you suppose they mean?

Japanese: 交通事故で死亡した女性とは、 この人物だったのでは‥‥？
English: Do you suppose the woman who died in that accident could be...?

Japanese: この人だったとしたら‥‥？
English: Do you suppose it could be this person...?

Japanese: ありえないわね。
English: Like, no way.
...Huh?

Japanese: 事故は１年前に起こったのよ。 そんな人、カンケイないじゃない。
English: Like, the accident was, like, a year ago, so, like, what's this person got to do with it?

Japanese: バカの相手をするシュミはないわ。 ‥‥サヨナラ。
English: I'm totally not interested in stupid people like you. Bye-bye...
