Japanese: （‥‥なんか、 開きなおったぞ、この女）
English: (...Ack, I have to stall her!)

Japanese: ‥‥待ってください！ もう、他には考えられない！
English: W-Wait! Look, I think I know who it is!

Japanese: 葉中　未実。 ‥‥あなたのお姉さんだった。
English: I believe it was your sister, Mimi Miney.

Japanese: ‥‥お姉さん‥‥。
English: Mimi...

Japanese: あなたは、お姉さんが 運転する車に乗っていた。
English: You were riding in your sister's car while she drove.

Japanese: ２人は、いっしょに 交通事故にあったんです！
English: The two of you were together when your accident occurred, making it one and the same!

Japanese: これなんですけど‥‥。
English: Would you take a look at this?

Japanese: あ。そういえば、国会議員の みなさんて、そういうバッジを‥‥
English: Hey! It's, like, one of those little badges people in Congress all wear...

Japanese: いやいやいや。 ‥‥だから、弁護士ですよ！
English: No, no, no. I'm a lawyer. L-A-W-Y-E-R!!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥？ そうでしたっけ。
English: Like, so is that what you are?

Japanese: やっぱり、ホンモノ だったんですねえ、倉院流って。
English: Wow, so, like, the Kurain technique is the real deal.

Japanese: 今度、大学のレポートに 書かなくちゃ。
English: I'm gonna, like, have to write about it in, like, my report for school.

Japanese: あ。そのときはこの写真、 おねがいしますね。
English: Hey, so, like, you'll give me this picture for, like, the report, right?

Japanese: ダメですよ！
English: No way!

Japanese: ごめんなさあい。
English: Like, I'm really sorry, but...

Japanese: せっかくですけどお‥‥ いりません、そんなの。
English: And I know, like, you totally had your heart set on it, but I can't, like, take it.

Japanese: （ダレがあげるって 言ったよ！）
English: (Who said anything about giving this to you!?)

Japanese: ホント、オキノドクですねえ。
English: She's, like, in a lot of trouble, huh?

Japanese: （ニコニコしながら しげしげ写真をながめている）
English: (She's stealing quick glances at the picture while smiling like a cheshire cat...)

Japanese: これで、霊媒が世間的に 認知されればシメたものですけど。
English: But, like, this will totally prove the power of channeling.

Japanese: （‥‥シメたもの‥‥って、 ナニが？）
English: (..."This"? What "this" is she talking about...?)

Japanese: ああ、綾里　キミ子さん。 おセワになってますう。
English: That's Morgan Fey. Like, she's been totally helpful.

Japanese: （‥‥って、写真に向かって おじぎされてもなあ‥‥）
English: (Did she just bow to the picture...?)

Japanese: で、このヒトが何か？
English: So what about her?

Japanese: いや、それを聞きたいんですけど。
English: Oh, nothing in particular. I just wanted to know what you knew about her.

Japanese: そうですねえ‥‥。 いい人ですよ。
English: Um... Well, she's, like, a really good person.

Japanese: そうですか。
English: I see.

Japanese: とっても。
English: Like totally.

Japanese: はあ。 （‥‥もう、いいや）
English: OK. (This is going nowhere...)

Japanese: ‥‥このカオ‥‥ どこかで見たような‥‥。
English: Um... This face... Like, I think I've seen it somewhere before...

Japanese: え！ ‥‥じょ、冗談ですよね？
English: What!? ...Y-You're joking, right?

Japanese: やだなあ。 決まってるじゃないですかあ。
English: Ha ha, like, of course I am, silly!

Japanese: （よ‥‥よかった）
English: (Oh, thank goodness...)

Japanese: お姉さんのコト‥‥ もう、聞かないでくださあい。
English: Please, like, don't ask me about my sister anymore...

Japanese: （‥‥写真を ロクに見もしないな‥‥）
English: (...Yeah, I've probably showed her this picture more than enough times...)

Japanese: ‥‥たしか、真宵さんの イトコさん‥‥でしたよねえ。
English: Like, that's totally Mystic Maya's cousin, right?

Japanese: そうです。春美ちゃん。
English: Yes. Her name is Pearl.

Japanese: ふうん。この子が 次の家元、ですかあ‥‥。
English: Hmm? So, like, this little kid is, like, the next Master...?

Japanese: い、いやいや、ちがいますよ。 家元は真宵ちゃんです。
English: Oh, no, no, no. Maya is the next in line to be Master.

Japanese: あ。そ、そうでしたねえ‥‥
English: Ah, oh yeah. Like, that's right.

Japanese: ‥‥この人のことなんですけど。
English: What do you know about this person...?

Japanese: すみませえん。私、そろそろ おヒルネの時間だから‥‥。
English: I'm, like, sorry, but it's totally, like, time for my nap...

Japanese: ふわあ‥‥ （ぼくまでアクビしてどうする！）
English: *yawn* (Ack! She's even got ME yawning!)

Japanese: キョーミありませえん。 そんなヒト‥‥。
English: And I, like, totally don't care about that person anyway...

Japanese: こういう人物を 見かけませんでしたか？
English: Have you seen this woman?

Japanese: やだあ。いいトシして、 刑事さんごっこですかあ？
English: Like, aren't you, like, totally too old to be, like, playing Cops and Robbers?

Japanese: ‥‥そういうワケじゃ ないんですけどね‥‥。
English: ...We're not playing Cops and Robbers...

Japanese: その人なら、さっき “控えの間”へ行きましたよお。
English: Like, if you're looking for her, she, like, went towards the "Side Room".

Japanese: なんだか、泣きわめいて いましたケド‥‥。
English: But, like, she was crying about something.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・わたりろうか
English: June 21 Fey Manor Winding Way

Japanese: あらあ。‥‥おひさしぶりです。
English: Hey! Like, I haven't seen you in a while...

Japanese: どうも。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: （そろそろ、このヒトのヒミツが 見えてきたかな‥‥）
English: (I think I have enough to finally unlock her secret...)

Japanese: ‥‥なんですかあ。 コワいカオして‥‥
English: ...Like, what's with that scary look on your face...?

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・修験者の間
English: June 21 Fey Manor Meditation Room

Japanese: （ひっそりしている‥‥）
English: (It's really empty...)

Japanese: （今日は修験者たちの 修行があるって言ってたけど）
English: (There's supposed to be training held today,)

Japanese: （もう、 終わっちゃったのかな‥‥）
English: (but I guess it's already over...)

Japanese: ミゴトな筆さばきで ４つの文字がならんでいる。
English: Someone with a lot of skill wrote these four Chinese characters with a brush.

Japanese: これは‥‥“一期一会”か。
English: Oh look, here's a translation: "Once in a Lifetime."

Japanese: ‥‥霊との出会いを大切に しましょう、ということかな。
English: Its message is people should value their ghostly meetings as once in a lifetime chances.

Japanese: 霊を呼び出す“対面の間”に 通じる扉だ。
English: A door that leads to the Channeling Chamber, the place where spirits and people meet.

Japanese: 鉄ワクで強化されていて、 ものすごくがんじょうだ。
English: The iron in this door was tempered, making it very solid.

Japanese: どうやら、カギもかかるように なっているらしい。
English: But I guess it wasn't strong enough to withstand The Phoenix!

Japanese: もめんの布地に、きれいな 文字がビッシリ並んでいる。
English: A piece of cloth with a ton of finely-written characters jammed onto it.

Japanese: 霊媒師の心得みたいなものかな。 ‥‥どれどれ。
English: Probably esoteric knowledge only mediums would know. ...Hmm, let's see...

Japanese: タイトルは‥‥ “お金のたまる１００の方法”
English: Here's one in English... It says... "100 Ways to Save Money".

Japanese: 霊媒師の生活は、 やはりラクではないみたいだ。
English: Being a medium sounds like a rough way of life...

Japanese: こっちは わたりろうかに通じている。
English: This door leads out to the Winding Way.

Japanese: そとはいい天気みたいだな。
English: The weather outside looks really nice.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・修験者の間
English: June 21 Fey Manor Meditation Room

Japanese: ‥‥ナツミさん！
English: Lotta...

Japanese: 出たァッ！
English: Aaack!

Japanese: あ、また‥‥！
English: H-Hey!

Japanese: たのむさかい、 追ってこんといてェ！
English: I'm tellin' ya, quit followin' me, ya hear!?

Japanese: ウチはダメな女や！ 見たらアカン！　目がクサるで！
English: I'm a worthless piece of trash! Ya lay eyes on me, and they'll turn ta dust!

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・修験者の間
English: June 21 Fey Manor Meditation Room

Japanese: あ。なるほどくん。 今、あの人が走っていきましたよ。
English: Oh, Mr. Nick! That person ran by here just now.

Japanese: あのひと？
English: What person?

Japanese: ほら。あの‥‥コゲた わたアメみたいなアタマの‥‥
English: Um, you know... The person with the fluffy cotton candy hair...

Japanese: （ああ、ナツミさんか）
English: (Ah, she means Lotta.)

Japanese: “そんな目で見んといてェ！” とおっしゃってましたけど、
English: She said, "Don't look at me with 'em eyes!" but...

Japanese: わたくし、そんなに 目つきが悪いのでしょうか‥‥
English: I'm not that scary, am I...?

Japanese: ‥‥たぶん、そういうコト じゃないと思うよ。
English: ...I don't think that's quite what she meant.

Japanese: あ。そうそう。 あのかたから伝言があります。
English: Oh, that's right. She also wanted me to tell you,

Japanese: “自分を見つめる旅に出るので、 探さないでくれ”だそうです。
English: "Goin' on a trip to find myself, so don't ya bother findin' me!"

Japanese: そ、そう。ありがと。
English: O-Oh... Well, thanks.

Japanese: どういたしまして。
English: You're welcome.

Japanese: あの日、 真宵さまの霊媒が始まったころ‥‥
English: Around when Mystic Maya started the channeling on that day,

Japanese: ろうかであそんでいたんです。 その‥‥マリで。
English: I was playing in the Winding Way... with my ball.

Japanese: それで、あのツボを‥‥？
English: And that's when you... broke the urn?

Japanese: は‥‥はい。 わ、割ってしまって‥‥。
English: Y-Yes... I broke it...

Japanese: キョウコさまの魂を‥‥ 逃がしてしまったのです！
English: I... I accidentally let Mystic Ami's spirit fly away!

Japanese: キミ子さんには 知らせなかったんだ。
English: Don't worry. Morgan will never know, OK?

Japanese: わたくし、わるい子です‥‥。
English: I'm a bad child...

Japanese: 里の宝物をこわしては、 ただではすまないでしょう。
English: Breaking a village treasure is unforgivable.

Japanese: もしかしたら、里を追放されて しまうかも‥‥そう思いました。
English: I thought... that maybe they would make me leave the village.

Japanese: でも、さいわい、 ツボのカケラは大きくて‥‥
English: But the broken pieces were pretty big, so...

Japanese: 気がつかれないように なおせる、って思ったんだ。
English: You thought you could put them back together, and no one would notice?

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: ...Yes.

Japanese: わたくし、接着剤をもってきて、 ろうかで‥‥なおしたのです。
English: I found some glue and brought it to the hallway... and fixed the urn there.

Japanese: あの、わたりろうかで？
English: The hallway? You mean "Winding Way"?

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes.

Japanese: もう霊媒は始まっていましたので、 だれも来ないと思いました。
English: The channeling had already started, so I didn't think anyone would walk by.

Japanese: ふうん‥‥。
English: Hmm, I see...

Japanese: で、どれぐらいかかったの？ ‥‥ヒドいしあがりだけど。
English: So, about how long did it take you? It must have been a nasty repair job.

Japanese: わたくし、おさいほうは トクイなのですが‥‥
English: Yes. I might be strong at spiritual things,

Japanese: お絵かきや工作は、 ちょっとニガテなのです。
English: but when it comes to arts and crafts, I am not nearly as skilled.

Japanese: 修理が終わって目を上げたら、 なるほどくんが、お屋敷に‥‥。
English: When I had finished fixing the urn and looked up, I saw you coming into the manor...

Japanese: （ちょうど、ぼくとナツミさんが 事件を通報したとき、か‥‥）
English: (So that's around when Lotta and I were coming back inside after reporting the murder.)

Japanese: それを見て、おおいそぎで おかたづけをしました。
English: When I saw that, I packed everything up in a hurry.

Japanese: あの‥‥なるほどくん？
English: Um... Mr. Nick?

Japanese: ん？
English: What is it, Pearls?

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥、 追放でしょうか？　わたくし。
English: I'm... I'm going to get banished, aren't I?

Japanese: ‥‥そんなことないと思うよ。
English: I really don't think that's going to happen.

Japanese: せまいツボから出られて、 キョウコさんもよろこんでるよ。
English: I mean, I bet Mystic Ami's happy to be free from that cramped urn.

Japanese: そ、そうだといいですね‥‥
English: Y-Yeah, I hope so...

Japanese: （ワレながら、 キレイにまとめたな‥‥）
English: (No harm done, and a happy resolution... Phew!)

Japanese: 証拠品《倉院のツボ》の データを書きなおした。
English: Sacred Urn updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: ～春美のアリバイ～
English: -- Pearl's Alibi --

Japanese: 春美ちゃん。
English: Pearl.

Japanese: 事件があったときのこと、 教えてもらうよ。
English: You have to tell me what you were doing when the murder happened, OK?

Japanese: あの‥‥どうしても 言わなくちゃダメなのですか？
English: Um... But what if I can't just tell you what happened...?

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、ぼくが 当ててみせようか。
English: Then I'll guess what happened. How does that sound?

Japanese: えっ！　そ、そんなことが できるのですか！
English: Eh!? Y-You can do that?

Japanese: 当たっていたら、 ちゃんと話してくれるね？
English: You'll tell me if I guess something right, won't you?

Japanese: 事件があったとき、 春美ちゃんはここにいたんでしょ？
English: Now, when the murder was taking place, you were here, right?

Japanese: ぼくはなんでも知ってるよ。 ‥‥ここにいたんだろう？
English: I'm pretty smart, Pearls. I know you were right here.

Japanese: ちがいます。
English: No, I wasn't...

Japanese: え。いきなりちがう？
English: Huh? I'm wrong?

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっぴり、ガッカリです。
English: Ooh, I feel a little better.

Japanese: （ちょっぴり、カオが赤くなった ‥‥考えなおそうかな）
English: (Ooh, I feel a little warmer... from embarrassment. ...I'd better try again...)

Japanese: 春美ちゃんは、ここにいたんだ。 この‥‥わたりろうかにね。
English: You were here, weren't you Pearls -- in the Winding Way?

Japanese: あっ、すごい！ ‥‥当たり、です。
English: W-Wow! ...Y-You got it.

Japanese: どう？ 話してくれる気になった？
English: So? Do you think you can tell me now?

Japanese: ‥‥す、すみません。
English: ...I'm sorry... Not yet...

Japanese: やれやれ。しかたないな。 じゃあ、次は‥‥
English: Man, oh man... Alright, what next...?

Japanese: 春美ちゃんが、そこで何を していたか、当ててみよう。
English: OK, I think I know what you were doing there.

Japanese: そ‥‥そんなことまで わかってしまうのですか？
English: D-Do you really know that much?

Japanese: 弁護士だからね。
English: Well, I am a lawyer, you know.

Japanese: あのとき、わたりろうかで 春美ちゃんは何をしていたか？
English: Now, what was it Pearls was doing in the Winding Way?

Japanese: どう？
English: So?

Japanese: ちょっと、安心しました。
English: I feel just a little better.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: ベンゴシといえども、 同じ人間ですものね‥‥。
English: You are a lawyer, but you're also still a person, after all...

Japanese: ゴメン。もう一度 やりなおすから。
English: Sorry, I'll get it right this time...

Japanese: このマリで‥‥ あそんでたんでしょ？
English: You were... playing with this ball, right?

Japanese: あっ！　あ、当たりです！ ‥‥ど、どうして‥‥
English: Ah! That's right! How... How did you know...?

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんが言ってたからね。 『はみちゃんは、マリ大好きっ子』
English: Maya told me that you love to play with your ball.

Japanese: そ‥‥そうです。わたくし、 マリであそんでいました。
English: Um... Yeah. I was playing with my ball.

Japanese: うんうん。それで？
English: Un, huh. And then?

Japanese: 何か、あったんだね？ ‥‥とんでもないコトが‥‥。
English: Something happened, huh? ...Something really bad...

Japanese: どきっ！
English: *gulp!*

Japanese: な、なんですか“何か”って！ わた、わたくしはッ‥‥！
English: Wh-What do you mean, "something"!? N-N-Nothing happened...!

Japanese: これが最後だよ。
English: This is it.

Japanese: マリであそんでいたとき‥‥ いったい、何が起こったか？
English: While you were playing with your ball, this happened!

Japanese: おどろきのあまり、 こえも出ない？
English: Were you so surprised that you were shocked into silence?

Japanese: ‥‥はい。あんなに自信たっぷり だったのに、はずすなんて‥‥。
English: ...Yes, at how wrong you are, even though you were so sure of yourself...
...Huh?

Japanese: （‥‥これ以上ないほど、カレイに ハジをかいたみたいだ）
English: (Way to embarrass yourself in front of an 8 year old, Wright...)

Japanese: ごめんごめん。 今度は冗談ぬきで行くから。
English: Ah, sorry, sorry. Just see if you were paying attention... Ah heh heh.

Japanese: そ、そうなのですか？
English: It's okay Mr. Nick. You can always try again.

Japanese: ‥‥このツボ、 里の宝物なんだってね。
English: This urn... It's a village treasure, right?
Ah... Um...

Japanese: 倉院流霊媒道の創始者の魂が 封じこめられているんでしょ？
English: And the spirit of the founder of the Kurain technique is sealed in here, isn't it?

Japanese: なんか、ヒビだらけだね。 それに、スキ間もあいてるし‥‥。
English: What's this? It's cracked all over, isn't it? There's even a few gaps here and there...

Japanese: うう‥‥ イジめないでください‥‥
English: Um... Please don't tease me...

Japanese: 春美ちゃん、割っちゃったんだね？ このツボを。
English: Pearl, you broke the Sacred Urn, didn't you?

Japanese: きっと、マリが当たったんだ。
English: The ball smacked into it and knocked it over, right?

Japanese: キミ子さんにバレたら、 怒られちゃうもんね。
English: And if Morgan found out, she'd get really mad, huh?

Japanese: （だまりこんじゃったぞ‥‥ 大当たり、みたいだな）
English: (She doesn't seem to be fighting back... I think I hit the bull's-eye this time.)

Japanese: （でも、ロックがパリーンと いかないのが気になるな）
English: (But it's strange that the lock hasn't broken yet...)

Japanese: ふ‥‥古いツボなのですよ、あれ。
English: I-It's... It's a really old urn, isn't it?

Japanese: え？
English: Eh?

Japanese: ひ‥‥ヒビぐらい入っていても お、おかしくないです‥‥
English: I-I-It's so old... So it's not that weird... to have a few cracks...

Japanese: （声がふるえてるよ‥‥）
English: (Her voice is shaking...)

Japanese: あのツボが一度割れたって、 どうしてわかるのですか！
English: H-How can you tell that urn has been broken!?

Japanese: ‥‥すみません。 ワガママ言ってしまって。
English: ...I'm sorry. I don't want to be mean, but...

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥ダメか‥‥）
English: (*sigh* I figured...)

Japanese: あの‥‥もう一度、 やってみましょうか？
English: Um, do you want to try again?

Japanese: うん。たのむよ。 （よく考えればわかるはずだぞ！）
English: Yeah. (I'm sure I can get it if I think about it some more!)

Japanese: （‥‥手がかりになるのは やっぱり‥‥）
English: (The thing I'm missing...)

Japanese: （ツボに書かれている文字、 しかないかな‥‥）
English: (must have something to do with what's written on the urn...)

Japanese: ‥‥倉院流霊媒道って、 だれが始めたんだっけ？
English: Who started the Kurain Channeling Technique?
Eh?

Japanese: キョウコさま‥‥ですけれど。
English: Um, it was Mystic Ami...

Japanese: その、キョウコさまって、 どう書くか知ってる？　漢字で。
English: That's right. And how do you spell her name? Do you know?

Japanese: かんじ‥‥ですか？ あの‥‥知りません。
English: How do I spell her name? Um... I don't know...

Japanese: “供子”って書くんだよ、きっと。
English: It's spelled "A-M-I".

Japanese: “供子”、ですか‥‥ ああああっ！
English: *gasp!*

Japanese: ツボを見て、フシギに思ったよ。 どうして“子供”なのか、って。
English: I thought it was a little odd when I saw "I AM" written on this urn.

Japanese: それでわかった。 だれかがあのツボを、割ったんだ。
English: And that's how I knew that someone must have broken this urn.

Japanese: そして、修理したときに‥‥ まちがえたんだ。
English: And made a mistake in spelling when they put it back together again...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・対面の間
English: June 21 Fey Manor Channeling Chamber

Japanese: （‥‥あれ、キミ子さんがいる。 ぼくには気づいてないみたいだ）
English: (...Hmm, looks like Morgan's here too, but she hasn't noticed me yet...)

Japanese: （何やら‥‥写真に向かって 語りかけているぞ‥‥）
English: (What is she doing...? She looks like she's talking to a picture...)

Japanese: ‥‥よろしくて？ 見ていてごらんなさい‥‥
English: ...Don't you see? Take a look around...

Japanese: やっと、チャンスが めぐってきた‥‥
English: Finally, my chance has come...

Japanese: この日を、どれだけ 待っていたことか！
English: I've waited for this day for so long!

Japanese: ‥‥カクゴなさいな。 ‥‥マイコ‥‥
English: That's right... Prepare yourself, dear Misty...

Japanese: （なんだって‥‥？）
English: (What in the...!?)

Japanese: だれッ！
English: Who's there!?

Japanese: ‥‥ああら。成歩堂さんじゃ ござあませんか‥‥。
English: ...Oh, if it isn't Mr. Wright...

Japanese: あ、あの‥‥。今、見ていた 写真ですけど‥‥
English: Um... Just curious, but that picture you were just looking at...

Japanese: ああた、アレですわねえ。 今日はごカツヤクだったとか。
English: Oh, today was that event, was it not, good sir? I heard you did well.

Japanese: え‥‥あ、はい。 ありがとうございます。
English: Huh, oh, yes... Thank you.

Japanese: アタクシ、法廷のほうへ行けなくて 申しわけござあませんでしたねえ。
English: I am terribly sorry I could not attend the trial today. Please accept my apology.

Japanese: ホレ、アレでござあますわ。 修験者たちを見ておりましたので。
English: I had a previous engagement, as I had to watch over the trainees' training today.

Japanese: （写真のこと、しゃべる気は ないみたいだな‥‥）
English: (I get the sense she really doesn't want to talk about the picture...)

Japanese: 家元がご不在でござあますから。
English: All because the Master can't be here with us, you understand.

Japanese: アタクシのような分家の者が、 ケイコをつけておりますの。
English: And that is why a lowly branch family member such as myself must do this.

Japanese: “修験者の間”に通じる扉。 ずいぶん重そうだ。
English: The Meditation Room is on the other side of this very heavy-looking door.

Japanese: 鉄の錠前がコワれている。 ぼくがやったんだけど‥‥。
English: The lock on it is broken, because, well... I broke it.

Japanese: ムチュウだったとはいえ、 よくコワせたな。
English: It doesn't seem possible, but I managed to break it pretty badly.

Japanese: 人間、やる気になれば なんでもできるんだね。
English: When we want to do something bad enough, people can do the most amazing things.

Japanese: 霊媒師がすわる場所だろうか。 タタミが４枚、しかれている。
English: This is where the medium sits, I assume. There are 4 panels of this flooring.

Japanese: 空気がよどんでいるせいか、 ちょっとジメジメしている。
English: The straw in the flooring feels a little damp, probably from the humidity in the air.

Japanese: ‥‥どうしても、血のあとに 目が行ってしまうな‥‥。
English: No matter what I do, my eyes keep getting drawn to the pool of blood in the center here.

Japanese: 霊媒師がすわる場所だろうか。 タタミが４枚、しかれている。
English: This is where the medium sits, I assume. There are 4 panels of this flooring.

Japanese: 空気がよどんでいるせいか、 ちょっとジメジメしてるな。
English: The straw in the flooring feels a little damp, probably from the humidity in the air.

Japanese: 祭壇‥‥というのだろうか。 重いフンイキがただよっている。
English: I guess this is an alter. Whatever it is, it feels very important.

Japanese: 表面がうすくけむった カガミのようなものや、
English: A mirror-like object with a cloudy reflective surface,

Japanese: 青あおとしたサカキが まつられている。
English: and some branches of a sacred tree have been set in a pre-determined arrangement.

Japanese: 霊媒に使うのかな ‥‥やっぱり。
English: I'd guess they're used during the channeling...

Japanese: ビョウブのうしろは 床の間になっているようだ。
English: There is some empty space behind the folding screen.

Japanese: ローソクのあかりも とどかず、まっくらだな。
English: The light of the candles can't reach back there, so it's completely dark.

Japanese: 事件のあったとき、 ここに人影はなかった‥‥
English: There wasn't anyone back here at the time of the murder...

Japanese: そう、ナツミさんは 証言していたけど。
English: Or that's what Lotta testified in court...

Japanese: （ぼくも見ておけばよかった‥‥）
English: (I knew I should've taken a look... I can always count on Lotta to make things hard...)

Japanese: ずいぶん古いビョウブだ。 ふちがボロボロになっている。
English: This folding screen is really showing its age. All of its edges are a bit tattered.

Japanese: くずした字体で、呪文のような ものが書かれている。
English: The lettering is fading, too. I think I recognize a few of the characters on here...

Japanese: ‥‥ダメだ。 ゼンゼン読めないや。
English: I'm pretty sure that's what it says... Maybe I'll ask Maya about it later...

Japanese: 毎日やってるんですか？ 修験者さんたちの修行‥‥。
English: So do the students train every day?

Japanese: そうでござあますわね。
English: Yes, absolutely.

Japanese: 事件があった次の日だけは、 お休みいたしましたけど‥‥。
English: They were given a day off the day after the murder, of course, however...

Japanese: その修行には、やっぱり 真宵ちゃんや春美ちゃんも‥‥？
English: And I assume both Maya and Pearls went through this training...?

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂さん！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: は、はい！ （急にコワくなるんだよな‥‥）
English: Ack! Yes, yes! (Wow, she got real scary, real fast...)

Japanese: ああた、何度言えば おわかりになるんですか。
English: How many times must I tell you before you understand, good sir?

Japanese: 真宵“サマ”に、春美“サマ” でござあます。
English: They are to be addressed as Mystic Maya and Mystic Pearl.

Japanese: す、すみません。真宵サマと、 春美サマ‥‥って。
English: S-Sorry... I meant Mystic Maya and Mystic Pearl...

Japanese: あ、あの‥‥春美ちゃんもですか？
English: Wait... I'm sorry, but "Mystic" Pearl? Isn't that going a bit too far...?

Japanese: ついで、でござあます。
English: Next question, please.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥。 ヒドいことになったな）
English: (Oh man... She's really being a pain in the butt about this...)

Japanese: クチのききかたには お気をつけなさいな。
English: Please remember to watch not only what you say, but how you say it, good sir.

Japanese: 家元‥‥って、真宵ちゃんの おかあさん、なんですよね？
English: So the Master is... um, Maya's mother, right?

Japanese: ‥‥舞子（まいこ）さま。 すばらしい霊媒師でござあます。
English: ...Mystic Misty... She is a brilliant medium.

Japanese: その家元は‥‥ 今、どちらに？
English: And where is she now...?

Japanese: 行方不明でござあます。
English: We are not sure.

Japanese: ‥‥行方不明‥‥。
English: Not sure?

Japanese: 今から１６、７年前。
English: About 16 or 17 years ago,

Japanese: 舞子さまは、ある霊媒に 失敗されたのでござあます。
English: Mystic Misty failed at channeling a certain spirit.

Japanese: それ以来‥‥すがたを 消してしまわれました。
English: And after that... she simply disappeared.

Japanese: おそらく、もう二度と 舞子さまはもどられますまい。
English: I don't believe she will return to this village, in any case.

Japanese: ４年後、舞子さまの名前は、 この里から消えるのでござあます。
English: And in four years' time, her name will be forever erased from this village.
...Erased?

Japanese: 里を２０年あけた者はだれであれ、 死んだものとされますから‥‥。
English: A person who has been away from the village for over 20 years is considered dead...

Japanese: そうなんですか。
English: Oh, I see.

Japanese: 今から４年後。
English: And that is four years from now for her.

Japanese: 倉院の里には、新しい家元が 生まれるのでござあます。
English: When that happens, a new Master will be appointed to Kurain Village.

Japanese: それって‥‥？
English: And...?

Japanese: このままいけば、真宵さま ‥‥だったのでござあますが。
English: Mystic Maya was supposed to become the next Master...

Japanese: ‥‥あんな事件を 起こされてしまってはねえ‥‥
English: However with this... murder, I'm afraid...

Japanese: （気のせい‥‥かな。 笑っているような‥‥）
English: (I must be imagining things... I thought she was laughing to herself for a second there...)

Japanese: ‥‥何か思い出したコト、 ありませんか？
English: Have you remembered something about the murder?

Japanese: そうでござあますわねえ‥‥。
English: No, I told you everything I know already.

Japanese: ぼくたちが警察を呼びに行った あいだは、ずっと‥‥？
English: Where you in this room the whole time when Lotta and I went to call the police?

Japanese: ええ。もちろん、 真宵さまのそばにおりました。
English: Yes, of course. I was by Mystic Maya's side the entire time.

Japanese: 除霊の術をほどこして‥‥ 介抱してさしあげて。
English: I performed the Spirit Severing Technique and held her in my arms afterward.

Japanese: それはきのうも聞きました。 ほかに、変わったことは？
English: Hmm, you said that yesterday as well. Did anything else happen?

Japanese: そう言われましても、 アレでござあますわねえ‥‥。
English: I would like to say more did, however that is all that occurred.

Japanese: （新しい材料が ほしいんだけどな‥‥）
English: (Ah, I was really hoping for some new info...)

Japanese: ‥‥ござあませんわね。 ごめんあそばせ。
English: I suppose nothing happened in the end, good sir. Sorry to have played on your hopes.

Japanese: （‥‥なんだか、バカにされてる ような気がするな‥‥）
English: (Why do I feel like I was just made fun of in the worse way possible...?)

Japanese: 倉院流霊媒道の呪詞、 全六葉でござあます。
English: There are six parts to the Kurain Sacred Writings.

Japanese: この里の宝物で ござあますのに‥‥
English: To think that this valued treasure of our village...

Japanese: 真宵さまも、ピストルで撃って しまわれるとは‥‥
English: and Mystic Maya were both shot by a gun...

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥
English: Well, that's...

Japanese: たとえ霊媒中のコトとは言え、 ユルされることではござあません。
English: Even if it was something that occurred during a channeling, it is an unforgivable act.

Japanese: それは‥‥この部屋のカギ！
English: This... This is the key to this room!

Japanese: ‥‥そうですね。
English: That's right.

Japanese: ど、どうして ああたが、そんなものを！
English: B-But why do you have it, good sir!?

Japanese: それは、言えません。
English: That, I'm not telling.

Japanese: お‥‥お返しなさいッ！
English: R-Return it at once!

Japanese: だ‥‥ダメですよ。 証拠品ですから。
English: I... I can't. It's evidence, after all.

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら、このカギの 重要性がわかっているらしいな）
English: (I think she knows exactly how important this key is to this murder...)

Japanese: （でも、なんだろう。 この感じは‥‥殺気？）
English: (But what is this feeling I'm getting from her? It feels... Hmm... I can't describe it.)

Japanese: 真宵さまの装束で ござあますわね‥‥。
English: That is Mystic Maya's costume, isn't it...?

Japanese: まさか‥‥あんなことに なってしまうとは‥‥
English: It's a shame. I never thought anything like that would happen to her...

Japanese: まだ、真宵ちゃんがやったと 決まったわけでは‥‥
English: But we don't know that it was Maya that did it...

Japanese: オキノドクな真宵さま。
English: Oh, poor Mystic Maya.

Japanese: （聞いてないなー、ヒトの話）
English: (Do you ever listen to anyone other than yourself?)

Japanese: 除霊する前の真宵さま ‥‥でござあますね。
English: That is Mystic Maya before I applied the Spirit Severing Technique...

Japanese: アタクシにコトワリもなく、 こんな写真を‥‥。
English: This picture... It was taken without my express permission.

Japanese: やはり、あんな女は 入国させてはならなかったのです。
English: I knew allowing a person like her from the "Heartland" into our country was a bad idea.

Japanese: （ナツミさん、ニッポン人 なんだけどな‥‥いちおう）
English: (You do realize that the "Heartland" is a part of this country, right...?)

Japanese: どうでしょう、これ‥‥。
English: What do you think about this?

Japanese: 申しわけござあませんが、
English: I am terribly sorry, but

Japanese: アタクシ、霊力はさほど 強くござあませんので‥‥
English: I am afraid my spiritual powers are not as strong as you might think...

Japanese: なんとも申せませんですわねえ。
English: I really am terribly sorry.

Japanese: （霊力はカンケイないと 思うけど‥‥）
English: (Um, but this has nothing to do with your spiritual power...)

Japanese: この人ですけど‥‥。
English: About this person...

Japanese: ああ。アレでござあましょ？
English: Oh! It is that man, is it not?

Japanese: 死者のチカラを借りて 自分の無力さをかくそうとして、
English: A man who wishes to borrow a departed spirit's power to hide the lack of his own.

Japanese: そのあげく、お亡くなりになった ちっぽけな殿方。
English: What an insignificant man. And see how pitifully he died.

Japanese: ち、ちっぽけ‥‥。
English: Insignificant...?

Japanese: ああたは、その殿方の ちっぽけなお友だち。
English: That would mean you are that man's insignificant little friend, dear sir.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんは、そのぼくの ちっぽけな助手、ってところか）
English: (Then I guess that makes Maya my insignificant little assistant, huh?)

Japanese: そういえば、今日はあの子、 見かけませんでしたわねえ。
English: Come to think of it, I don't believe I have seen my child today.

Japanese: ああた、ご存じありませんか。
English: Would you happen to know where she is, good sir?

Japanese: ご、ご存じありません。
English: N-No, I wouldn't.

Japanese: もう。修行を１日休むだけでも、 とりもどすのはタイヘンなのに。
English: Honestly! Take one day off from training, and it is neigh impossible to make it up.

Japanese: ああたッ！　くれぐれも ヘンなあそびを教えませんように。
English: Good sir! I pray you are not teaching her strange, fiendish things in her time off!

Japanese: （‥‥ケッキョク 怒られるんだよな‥‥）
English: (Oh boy. Here comes her anger. ...And we were having such a civil conversation too...)

Japanese: ああたッ！
English: Good sir!

Japanese: は、はいッ！
English: Y-Yes!

Japanese: いつの間にそんな‥‥
English: When did you...

Japanese: 真宵さまを かくし撮りするなんて！
English: take a hidden picture of Mystic Maya!?

Japanese: そ、そ、そんなんじゃ ないですよ！
English: Wh-Wh-What!? Wait... It's not like that at all!

Japanese: ユルしませぬ！
English: It is unforgivable!

Japanese: いてて‥‥。 （ひっぱたかれた‥‥）
English: Ow-ow-ow! (Why did she slap me?)

Japanese: ‥‥はて。 どこかで見たような‥‥。
English: ...Oh, my... I think I may know of this person from somewhere...

Japanese: 千尋さんですよ。 真宵ちゃんの、お姉さん。
English: It's Mia. You know, Maya's older sister?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥！　あ、ああ。 そうでござあました。
English: Aah! Yes, that is who she is...

Japanese: （‥‥家元のムスメだろ。 ふつう、忘れるか‥‥？）
English: (...How could you forget a daughter of the Master...!?)

Japanese: たしか、弁護士になられたとか。 スバラシイことでしたわねえ。
English: If memory serves, she became a lawyer. Oh, how splendid for her, don't you think?

Japanese: （あの、ヤサシイほほえみ‥‥）
English: (That soft, kind smile...)

Japanese: （どうも、何かウラが ありそうなんだよな‥‥）
English: (It's the kind that tells you a pair of devil horns are not too far away...)

Japanese: 綾里の家に生まれながら、 霊力が弱い‥‥。
English: Being born as one of the Fey family, but of weak spiritual power...

Japanese: この里では、人として あつかってもらえませんわ。
English: It makes the people of this village look down upon you, you know?

Japanese: そ‥‥そうなんですか？
English: I-I didn't know that...

Japanese: だいじょうぶです。 ‥‥春美ちゃんさえいてくれれば。
English: But I will be alright. ...As long as I have Pearl.

Japanese: アタクシには、春美ちゃんが いてくれるのでござあますから。
English: I am satisfied because I have my Pearl.

Japanese: あ‥‥、キミ子さん！
English: Hey! Morgan!

Japanese: なんでござあますか。 ‥‥そうぞうしい。
English: Yes, what is it? ...And must you be so noisy!?

Japanese: “ろうかは走るべからず” ‥‥この里のおきてです。
English: We have a rule here: "The hallway is not for running"

Japanese: どうか、お静かに。
English: Now then, in a calm manner ask your question.

Japanese: あの‥‥こっちに ナツミさん、来ませんでしたか？
English: Okay... Anyway, did you see Lotta come by here?

Japanese: ああ。あの外国人のカタ‥‥。
English: Ah, that girl from the "Heartland"...

Japanese: こちらには まいりませんでしたわよ。
English: I'm sorry, but she did not pass by here at any time.

Japanese: （まったく、 手間のかかるヒトだな‥‥）
English: (Grr... Lotta! You sure know how to waste a man's time...)

Japanese: ‥‥このカタが、 どうかなされて‥‥？
English: ...What is it that this person has done?

Japanese: よくわからないんですけど、 今、なりゆきで探してるんです。
English: I don't know yet, but I'm going to find out... Once I find her, that is...

Japanese: このあたりでは 見かけませんでしたわねえ。
English: I have not seen her here in this area. Sorry.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・控えの間
English: June 21 Fey Manor Side Room

Japanese: （今日もフトンが ふくらんでいるな）
English: (The bedding is all laid out today too.)

Japanese: （とりあえず、そっと しておいたほうがいいな）
English: (Guess I'll leave them alone for now...)

Japanese: ‥‥すみません‥‥
English: ...Excuse me...

Japanese: （なんだ。だれも 寝ていないや‥‥）
English: (Who am I talking to? There's no one here.)

Japanese: そういえば、障子を見るのも ひさしぶりだな。
English: It's still charming to see a real sliding door like this in person.

Japanese: ここから、わたりろうかと 中庭が見える。
English: From here, I can see the Winding Way and the little garden in the courtyard.

Japanese: 床の間。‥‥ぼくにはどうも、 この空間のイミがわからない。
English: It's a small alcove. I have no idea what it's supposed to mean or symbolize.

Japanese: 事務所にこんなものがあったら、 あっという間に物置だな。
English: If I had one of these in the office, it would end up as storage space.

Japanese: しかしここには、きれいな花と シャレた掛け軸がかざってある。
English: But here, it's decorated with some pretty flowers and a beautiful hanging scroll.

Japanese: これは‥‥クマの置物だな。 台座にプレートがついているぞ。
English: It's a decorative wooden bear sculpture. There's a plate attached to the base.

Japanese: “霊媒の里・倉院” ‥‥なんだ、おみやげ品か。
English: "Kurain Village - The Heart of Channeling" What the!? It's a souvenir.

Japanese: ‥‥ってことは、クマが出るのか？ このへん‥‥。
English: So does this mean it's easy to spot bears here... And for them to spot you...? *gulp*

Japanese: これこれ。 さっきから気になってたんだ。
English: Hmm, I keep wondering about this box.

Japanese: たしか、事件が起こる前は 置いてなかったよな。
English: I'm sure this wasn't here before the murder took place.

Japanese: どうやら、衣装ばこ‥‥ のようだ。ずいぶん大きい。
English: It looks like a box for storing clothes... But it's pretty big for just clothes...

Japanese: ‥‥ほとんどカラだけど、 装束が数枚、たたまれている。
English: It's mostly empty, but a few folded pieces of channeling costumes sit at the bottom.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 綾里家・控えの間
English: June 21 Fey Manor Side Room

Japanese: ナツミさん‥‥こっちの方へ 来たはずなんだけど
English: I'm pretty sure I saw Lotta run in this direction...

Japanese: ‥‥どこかにかくれてるのかな。
English: but it doesn't look like she's here...

Japanese: 春美ちゃんのマリだ。
English: It's Pearl's ball.

Japanese: いつもは衣装ばこに しまっている、って‥‥
English: Maya said that it's always kept in the clothing box...

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんが言ってたけど。 ‥‥なんでこんなところに‥‥？
English: So then, what is it doing on the floor...?

Japanese: 《春美のマリ》をひろった。
English: Pearl's Ball taken.

Japanese: マリがしまわれていた 衣装ばこだ。
English: It's the clothing box where Pearl's ball is usually kept.

Japanese: 開けてみるか‥‥。
English: I wonder what's inside...

Japanese: ぎゃあっ！
English: Aaaaah!

Japanese: うわっ！
English: Whaaaa!?

Japanese: なな、ナニしてるんですか！ こんなところに入って！
English: Wh-Wh-What were you doing in there!? What were you thinking!?

Japanese: ウチはダメな女なんやッ！ ホンマ、穴があったら入りたいワ！
English: I'm a bad person! Just another piece of human trash. I needed a hole to crawl into...

Japanese: （アナがなかったから、 衣装ばこに入ってたワケか）
English: (So you crawled into this clothing box because there wasn't a hole to crawl into?)
(Oh man... ...Huh?)

Japanese: この衣装ばこ。 小さな穴があいてるぞ‥‥。
English: There's a small hole in this clothing box...

Japanese: 高さは‥‥床から、２０ｃｍ！
English: And it's about... 8 inches off the ground!

Japanese: あのビョウブと同じ高さだ！ もしかして、これは‥‥
English: That's the same height as the hole in the folding screen! Then, could this...?

Japanese: 証拠品《衣装ばこ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Clothing Box added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 春美ちゃんが、マリを しまっている衣装ばこだ。
English: It's the clothing box where Pearls keeps her ball.

Japanese: 中からナツミさんが出てきた ときには、死ぬかと思ったぞ。
English: I thought I felt my soul shake when Lotta jumped out at me.

Japanese: ヒトが１人、入れるほど 大きかったとはね‥‥。
English: So it's big enough to fit a grown adult... Well, if she can be called an adult...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 堀田クリニック　ロビー
English: June 21 Hotti Clinic Reception

Japanese: （‥‥ここが、のどかさんが 入院してた病院か‥‥）
English: (...So, this is where Ini was hospitalized...)

Japanese: ん。ご面会かな？　ん。
English: Hmm, yes... Are you here to visit a patient? Hmm...

Japanese: あ。どうも‥‥。 先生は、ここの‥‥？
English: Ah, yes... Are you a doctor here...?

Japanese: ん。院長の堀田（ほった） ですわ。ほっほっ。
English: Hmm, yes... I'm Director Hotti... Hoh, hoh.

Japanese: （うわ、いきなり 院長先生と対面か‥‥）
English: (Ack! I didn't think I'd be speaking with the Director himself...)

Japanese: ん。どうしたかな？　ん？
English: Hmm, yes...? So what can I help you with? Uh, huh...

Japanese: あの。葉中　のどかさんの ことなんですけど‥‥。
English: I'd like to ask you a few questions about Ms. Ini Miney.

Japanese: ん。葉中？　む。のどか、と。 ‥‥んんん‥‥。ああ、あの！
English: Hmm? Ms. Miney? Ms. Ini Miney? ...Hmm... Yes... Hmm... OH! Her!

Japanese: ザンネンだな。ん。
English: Ah, it's too bad.

Japanese: 彼女はもう、その。 ずいぶん前に退院しちまったよ。
English: I'll tell you something. That girl left here a long time ago.

Japanese: コラ！　おじいちゃん！
English: Here you are! Sir!

Japanese: ダメでしょ！　病室を出ちゃ。 ホラホラ、早くもどりなさい！
English: You know you shouldn't be up and about! Come on, it's back to your room with you!

Japanese: あ。マチコくん。ま。ま。 カタいこと言いっこなし。な。
English: Ah, Sweet'ums! Calm down, calm down. Don't be so harsh to an old man like me.

Japanese: なにが“マチコくん”よ！
English: Don't you dare start that "Sweet'ums" thing with me!

Japanese: いいかげんにしないと、 病院からほっぽり出すよ！
English: If you don't behave, you'll get yourself tossed out of this hospital!

Japanese: （ひゃあ‥‥院長に向かって、 スゴい看護師だな‥‥）
English: (Wow. I didn't think nurses could be this direct or forceful towards their boss.)

Japanese: いい？　院長先生の白衣、 ちゃんと返しておくのよ！
English: Come now. Be a good boy and give me the Director's white lab coat!

Japanese: はいはい。了解。 りょーかいさん。ん。
English: OK, OK. I got it. I. Got. It. Hmm, yes...

Japanese: あの。
English: Um...

Japanese: ん。ナニかな？　ん。
English: Hmm, yes? Uh, huh?

Japanese: ん。じゃありませんよ。 あなた、いったいダレなんですか？
English: Don't "Hmm, yes" me. Who in the world are you?

Japanese: 院長の堀田ですわ。ほっほっ。
English: I'm Director Hotti. Hoh, hoh.

Japanese: （看護師さんは行っちゃったし‥‥ 相手してくれそうなのは‥‥）
English: (The nurse went off some- where... Oh the inhumanity. Only person I can talk to...)

Japanese: （このヒトだけか。 ‥‥とほほ‥‥）
English: (...is this guy... ...Uuuugh...)

Japanese: のどか嬢、手術してから 治るまで、半年はかかったな。
English: It's been half a year since Miss Ini had her surgery and got better.

Japanese: 去年の１２月ごろ、 退院していったよ。
English: She was discharged sometime in December last year.
(It's already been half a year, huh...?)

Japanese: ん。ホレ、どうよ。 けっこう役に立ったろ？　ん？
English: Hmm, yes... So, how was I? I was a real help, wasn't I? Hmm, yes...

Japanese: は、はい。 ありがとうございました。
English: Y-Yes... Thank you very much.

Japanese: ん。じゃ、ワシ。そろそろ 病室にもどるからさ。
English: Hmm, yes... Well, then, I'll be getting back to my room then.

Japanese: のどか嬢によろしくな。ん。
English: Tell Miss Ini I said hi, OK? Hmm, yes...

Japanese: （ぼくも行くとするか。ん）
English: (It's time for me to leave too, hmm, yes... Ack! OK, it's definitely time to leave!)

Japanese: この病院の受付だけど‥‥ だれもいないな。
English: This is the clinic's reception desk, but it doesn't look like there's anyone at the counter.

Japanese: カベに、“今月のコトバ” が張りだされている。
English: On the wall is this month's "Word to the Wise":

Japanese: 『ホネ折り損の、 くたびれもうけ』‥‥。
English: "Do something in vain, and your only reward is tiredness."

Japanese: 外科患者のシンケイを さかなでするコトバだな。
English: Hmm, not exactly the best thing to be telling your patients, is it?

Japanese: 待合室だろうか。
English: This must be the waiting area.

Japanese: 気合いを入れて ソファを並べてるワリに、
English: It's got the feeling of one and there are sofas lined up...

Japanese: 患者は少ないみたいだ。
English: but I don't see all that many patients.

Japanese: ん。患者は量じゃない、 質だからね。ん。
English: Hmm, yes... Well, it's the quality, not quantity that counts. Uh huh...

Japanese: （今のコトバ‥‥ 何か意味があるのか‥‥？）
English: (What in the world did that have to do with anything...?)

Japanese: 患者さんが リハビリテーションをしている。
English: This patient is undergoing rehabilitation.

Japanese: カオを苦しそうにゆがめて、 アブラ汗がだらだら。
English: And look at that strained face twisting in all those ways... and that oily sweat... hmm...

Japanese: 全身がぶるぶるふるえて、 今にもたおれそうだ。ん。
English: His whole body is swaying back and forth, wobble-wobble, like he's about to fall. Hmm, yes.

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと！　カッテに しゃべらないでくださいよ！
English: Hey! Don't just cut into my monologue like that! I'm explaining things here!

Japanese: いや。スマン。ん。ゴメン。ん。
English: Ah, sorry. Hmm, yes... Most sorry... Yes... Uh, huh...

Japanese: （あの奥のドア‥‥ どこに通じてるんだろう？）
English: (I wonder where that door way down there leads to...?)

Japanese: あ。あれはな。 レントゲン室だよ。ん。
English: Oh, that's the X-ray room. Hmm, yes...

Japanese: な、なんでぼくの考えてる コト、見ぬいたんですか？
English: H-H-How did you know what I was thinking!?

Japanese: ん。そりゃ、ホレ。 レントゲン室だから。ん。
English: Hmm, yes... Well, it's... Because that's the X-ray room... Uh huh, yes...

Japanese: （いいのか？　このセンセイ‥‥）
English: (There is something very peculiar about this guy... Because I know it's not me...)

Japanese: あの。この病院は、 どういう‥‥？
English: So, what exactly do you treat at this clinic...?

Japanese: んん。その、外科一般を やっとるよ。ん。
English: Hmm, yes... We treat all sorts of... doctory, clinical stuff, uh, huh, yes...

Japanese: 外科一般‥‥？
English: D-Doctory, clinical stuff?

Japanese: あんまり詳しいコトは 知らんケドね。ほっほっ。
English: Well, I don't actually know or particularly care about the details. Hoh, hoh.

Japanese: ん。ん。なんだその目は。
English: Hmm, hmm... Yes... What is that look for?

Japanese: そ、そうだな。この近くに 大きな総合病院があるんだよ。ん。
English: A-Ah, yes. There's a really big general hospital around the corner. Hmm, yes...

Japanese: 患者が収容しきれないとき、 こっちに回されてくるんだよ。ん。
English: When they can't accommodate everyone, they move some of their patients here. Uh, huh.

Japanese: ここでは、外科手術を やってるんですよね？
English: You do surgeries here, right?

Japanese: ん。形成外科の手術なんぞも やってるみたいだけど。ん。
English: Hmm, yes... We do some plastic surgery here, it seems... Hmm...

Japanese: やってるみたい‥‥ですか。
English: It... seems?

Japanese: ん。ん。やってるみたいだよ。
English: Hmm, yes... Uh, huh... It seems that way.

Japanese: 葉中　のどかさんのことを 聞きたいんですけど‥‥。
English: What do you know about Ms. Ini Miney?

Japanese: きたッ！　ん。あの子ね。たしか、 総合病院から移されてきたんだよ。
English: Now you're talking! Hmm, yes. That girl, she was transferred from the general hospital.

Japanese: 総合病院から‥‥？
English: From the general hospital...? To here?

Japanese: 彼女、何か外科手術を 受けたんですか？
English: What kind of surgery did she need?

Japanese: ん。それ。外科手術。 タイヘンだったわ、アレは。
English: Hmm, yes... Well, she needed surgery. It was really bad. Really bad.

Japanese: どんな手術を‥‥？
English: So what kind was it...?

Japanese: ん。それは。いやいやいや。 ちょっとそれは。ん。言えんよ。
English: Hmm, yes... Well... No, no... You see, that is something... No, I can't tell you.

Japanese: （センセイ、ジツに うれしそうに笑ってるぞ‥‥）
English: (So you say with a happy smile on your face... "Mr. Director"...)

Japanese: ホレ。なんか、医者って連中は、 患者のコトしゃべっちゃイカンの。
English: See, there's this thing about how doctors aren't allowed to talk about patient stuff.

Japanese: な。な。テレビのドラマでも そうだろ？　ん？
English: It's in all those TV shows too, right? You've seen them, right? Hmm, yes?

Japanese: （このままじゃラチが あかないな‥‥）
English: (I'm never going to get him to unzip those broken zipper teeth of his at this rate...)

Japanese: （このジイさん‥‥ちょっと、 オドしつけてやれないかな？）
English: (There's got to be something I can show to this old coot...)

Japanese: のどかさんのこと、 聞かせてください。
English: Please tell me what you know about Ini Miney.

Japanese: ん。まかせなさい。 ほっほっ。まーかせなさい。
English: Hmm, yes... I'll get to that, trust me, I will.

Japanese: あの子は‥‥そう。１年ぐらい前、 総合病院からまわされてきたんだ。
English: That girl... That's right. She was transferred here from the general hospital.

Japanese: その。ホレ。急患っちゅうコトで。
English: This was... hmm, yes... a year ago. She was one of those... "emergency" cases.

Japanese: なんの病気だったんですか？
English: What was she ill with?

Japanese: いやいやいや。 ありゃ、ケガだったよ。
English: Ill? Nah, nah, nah. She was injured, not ill.

Japanese: ワシね、一目で見ぬいたよ。そりゃ もう、バッチリ。大ケガだ、って。
English: With my trained eye, I could see. Yep, I could see it was a huge wound at that.

Japanese: 大ケガ‥‥ですか。
English: A huge injury?

Japanese: カラダ中ホータイでぐるぐるまき。 ウデもギブスで固められてたしな。
English: Her whole body was wrapped around and 'round in bandages and her arm was in a cast.

Japanese: それはたしかに‥‥大ケガですね。
English: That sounds... pretty severe, alright...

Japanese: ん。事故だよ事故。コワいね。 やっぱりホレ。クルマはさ。ん。
English: Hmm, yes... It was an accident; an accident. They're scary, yes? Cars, hmm, yes...

Japanese: クルマ？ ‥‥交通事故だったんですか？
English: Car...? ...You mean it was a traffic accident?

Japanese: そうだよ。あれだけの大ケガだ、 きっと新聞にのってると思ってさ。
English: Yep! That's what gave her the terrible injuries. I swore it was in the papers, hmm...

Japanese: もう、ホレ。調べた調べた。 タイヘンだったね。ん。
English: Yes, I looked it all up, I did. It was terrible... Hmm, yes...

Japanese: （‥‥なんなんだ このジイさんは‥‥）
English: (...Dare I even ask what is wrong with this old guy...?)

Japanese: さっき‥‥、大手術だった、 って言ってましたね。
English: Just now... You mentioned something about a big operation?

Japanese: ん？　のどか嬢の手術かい？
English: Hmm? Are you talking about Miss Ini's operation?

Japanese: え、ええ。のどか嬢の。
English: Y-Yes... Miss Ini's.

Japanese: そりゃまあ、その。なんだ。 あれだけの事故だったし。
English: That... Ah, that was one heck of an accident.

Japanese: ん。カオのヤケドがさ。 特にヒドくて。タイヘンだったわ。
English: Hmm, yes... Her whole face was burned like bad toast. It was bad. Very bad.

Japanese: そんなに ヒドかったんですか‥‥。
English: Was it really that bad...?

Japanese: たまたま、のどか嬢が自分の写真を 持っていたからね。
English: Hmm, it was a good thing that Miss Ini had a picture of herself.

Japanese: それを見て、復元したらしいね。 カオをさ。
English: I heard that picture was how they put her face back together... Hmm...

Japanese: 写真‥‥
English: Picture...?

Japanese: 彼女さ。ホレ、なんだ。免許を とったばっかりだったんだな。
English: That girl, see, she had, uh... She had just gotten her license.

Japanese: その免許証のために撮った 写真を、ちょうど持ってたワケだ。
English: So she just happened to have a license photo on her, hmm, yes...

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: Oh.

Japanese: ワシね。記念にもらっちゃったよ。 その写真。コッソリと。
English: So, see, I got her picture. As a way to remember her, yes... Well, more like... Swiped...
(Um, breech of ethics...? ...Oh, wait...)

Japanese: な？　な？　よく撮れてるだろ？
English: What? What? A pretty girl like her -- I bet she has lots of pictures of herself...

Japanese: はあ‥‥。
English: Um... Sure...

Japanese: お宝だよ、お宝。ワシの。
English: It's a treasure. My treasure... Preciiiious...

Japanese: カンジンの免許証のほうは どうなったんですか？
English: What happened to the actual license itself?

Japanese: さあ。知らんよ。聞いてないな。 燃えちまったんじゃないかね。ん。
English: Who knows. I didn't bother to ask. It probably got burned up along with the car. Hmm, yes.

Japanese: 証拠品《免許用の写真》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: License Photo added to the Court Record.

Japanese: あの‥‥調べたんですよね、 のどかさんの事故のこと。
English: Um... So you "looked up" information on Ini's accident, correct?

Japanese: そりゃもう。そりゃね。ん。 当時の新聞はゼンブ見たぞ。
English: Yep, yep. Sure did... Hmm, yes... I read every paper that covered it at the time.

Japanese: ‥‥どんな事故だったか、 おぼえてますか？
English: What kind of accident was it, do you remember?

Japanese: ん。ちょっと待ってな。 今、持ってくるから。記事。
English: Hmm, yes... Hold on. I'll go get it for you. The article, I mean.

Japanese: え。 ‥‥も、持ってるんですか？
English: H-Huh? Y-You have it?

Japanese: ま。ま。ま。ま。ま。ま。ま。ま。 こんなコトもあろうかと思ってさ。
English: Well, well, well, don't get me wrong. I thought something like this might come up, so.

Japanese: （あの、あくなき探求心‥‥ 見ならうべきかも‥‥）
English: (Obsessive to the max... But I bet I could learn about how to stay on a lead from this guy.)

Japanese: ああ、これこれ。これだ。ん。
English: Ah, here we go, here we go... Hmm, yes...

Japanese: どうやらのどか嬢、 助手席に座ってたらしいな。
English: According to this, Miss Ini was in the passenger's seat.

Japanese: 助手席ですか‥‥。
English: The passenger's seat, huh?

Japanese: 眠っとったらしいよ。
English: And dozing... Or so it says.

Japanese: ものすごい衝撃で目がさめたら、 あたり一面、火の海だ。
English: A strong jolt woke her up, and the next minute, a sea of fire was all around her.

Japanese: のどか嬢、必死でドアを開けて、 なんとか自力で逃げたわけだ。
English: Miss Ini fought with the door, finally got it to open, and somehow managed to get away.

Japanese: ‥‥ま。くわしいコトはホレ。 この記事を読めば。ん。
English: ...Anywhoo, you can read all the details in the article. Hmm, yes...

Japanese: 証拠品《新聞記事・２》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Newspaper Clipping 2 added to the Court Record.

Japanese: （新しい手がかりだ！　目を とおしておいたほうがいいな）
English: (A new clue! I should probably look this over.)

Japanese: のどかさん‥‥そんな大事故に あっていたんですね。
English: Ini's accident sounds like it was really, really bad.

Japanese: そうとも。ん。
English: Sure was. Hmm, yes...

Japanese: カオをホレ、大ヤケドなんて、 キツかろうねオトメには。
English: Her face, you see, was really burned, and that's just deadly for a beautiful girl like her.

Japanese: でも‥‥、 キレイに治ってましたよ。
English: But... Didn't she have her face fixed up?

Japanese: そこがホレ、院長センセイの エラいとこだよ。ん。
English: Ah, that's because, that is, the Director is so great at surgery. Hmm, yes...

Japanese: あの、これなんですけど‥‥。
English: Um, so what do you know about this...?

Japanese: こんなの見てもさ。なんて言うか。 その。ん。ツマランな。ツマラン。
English: I see this and, um, my mind's blank. Hmm, yes. It's boring, that's why. Boring.

Japanese: ん。なんだ。ホレ。もっとこう、 ムラムラくるモンはないのかな。
English: Hmm, yes... How do I put it? If you had more... Hmm... More attractive items... Yes...

Japanese: （‥‥ムラムラ‥‥）
English: (...Attractive...? Huh?)

Japanese: おッ。こりゃなかなか。 シゲキ的な。うーん。イイ感じ。
English: Now this is what I'm talking about! Hmm, yes... nice. This is nice and exciting.

Japanese: どうですか？ 何か心当たりでも‥‥
English: What do you think? Does anything grab your attention?

Japanese: イイよこの写真。ん。 気に入っちゃったよ。ワシ。
English: There's no mistaking this is nice... Hmm, yes... I like... Yes... I like this picture...

Japanese: ちょっとコレ、ワシの胃薬と とっかえっこしないかね。ん。
English: Hmm, yes... Mind if I borrow this for a while...? For... "private research" purposes...

Japanese: ‥‥い、いいです。 （胃がイタいのはジジツだけど）
English: Umm, that's okay... (I don't think I should support his "research"...)

Japanese: うーん。やっぱりこれ、 のどか嬢、カワイイな。な？
English: Mmm... Miss Ini is really cute in this picture. You'd agree, right?

Japanese: は、はあ。
English: Y-Yeah, sure.

Japanese: もう一度、会いたいのお。 また大事故にあわんかね。ん。
English: I wish I could see her again. Maybe the next time she has a big accident. Hmm, yes...

Japanese: ‥‥本人に伝えておきます。
English: ...I'll tell her for you...

Japanese: ん。たのむよ。ん。 待っとるから、ワシ。
English: Hmm, yes... Please... Hmm... I'll be waiting for her... Hmm... Yes... Waiting...

Japanese: ん？　ナニかなそれは？
English: Hmm? What's this?

Japanese: ぼくは弁護士です。今、 ある事件の調査をしています。
English: I'm a lawyer. Right now, I'm investigating a murder.

Japanese: ん。ん。なんだコワいカオして。 ボーリョクはよくないぞ。ん。
English: Hmm, hmm, yes... What is with your angry face? Violence isn't the answer, hmm, yes...

Japanese: 裁判は明日です。 ‥‥情報がほしいんですよ。
English: The trial is tomorrow. And I would really like some information from you.

Japanese: む。そ、そうだったか。
English: Hmm... Is that right...?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥じつはな。そのう‥‥。 これはヒミツなんだが‥‥。
English: ...Well, you know, I think I can let you in on a little secret...

Japanese: なんですか？
English: What's that?

Japanese: ん。じつはワシ、 ‥‥院長じゃないんだコレが。
English: Hmm, yes... Actually, I'm not the real Director.

Japanese: 知ってますよそんなこと！
English: Are you kidding!? Anyone with half a fly's brain knew that!

Japanese: え！　な、なんで！
English: Eh!? Wh-What do you mean!?

Japanese: （カンゼンにナメられてる！）
English: (Do I have to explain this to him...?)

Japanese: ま。ま。待ちなさいって。 あげるから。ジョーホー。
English: Well, hmm... Hold on. I'll give you something. Real information, OK?

Japanese: ワシはな。この病院の、ホレ、 なんだ。‥‥ナマ字引？
English: I'm actually, this clinic's... See, I'm like this place's... "walking dictionpedia"...?

Japanese: 生き字引‥‥ですか？
English: You mean "walking encyclopedia"?

Japanese: それそれ。特に若い女のコちゃんの 患者なら知らんコトはない。
English: Y-Yeah, that. And my specialty is in young, silky-smooth hottie patients...

Japanese: いや、おハズかしい話。
English: There's nothing I don't know about them... But I get "embarrassed" talking about it.

Japanese: ‥‥ホントに ハズかしいですね。
English: ...Yeah... How about you keep that kind of information to yourself...?

Japanese: ほっほっほっ。とにかく、 葉中　のどか嬢のコトだったな。
English: Hoh, hoh, hoh. Anyway, you wanted to know about Miss Ini Miney, right?

Japanese: （のどか“嬢”‥‥）
English: ("Miss" Ini Miney?)

Japanese: あの子は特に、その。 よおくおぼえとるぞ。よおく、な。
English: That girl... Ah, I remember her especially well... Really, especially, very well...

Japanese: この子なんですけど‥‥。
English: So, about this girl...

Japanese: ‥‥ほお。なんというか。 これはなかなか。ん。
English: Isn't she the patient down in room 206...? Hmm, yes...

Japanese: ホネが折れたら、ゼヒ、 ウチにつれて来なさい。
English: I'm sure that cute nurse is taking very good care of her.

Japanese: そうだな。ころんでスリむいた ときも、かならずウチに。
English: That nurse said the girl has chicken pox... or was it measles... Maybe the flu...?

Japanese: （‥‥ゼッタイ つれて来るもんか‥‥！）
English: (Um, Pearls is fine and well, although I feel sorry for the nurse now.)

Japanese: この子なんですけど‥‥。
English: So about this girl...

Japanese: ‥‥うーん。なんというか。 これはイマイチ。ん。
English: Hmm... She is... Um... Not the best... Hmm, yes...

Japanese: まあ、このテイドなら、 手術にはおよばんね。ん。
English: But she'd be a great candidate for surgery... Hmm, yes, yes...

Japanese: ん。そうとも。 自然が解決してくれるだろ。
English: Hmm... That's really the only thing I can prescribe for her...

Japanese: （‥‥いったい なんのハナシだ‥‥？）
English: (...What is he talking about? What surgery...?)

Japanese: このかたなんですけど‥‥。
English: Would you please take a look at this person...?

Japanese: ‥‥おおお。これはこれは。 ふむふむ。スバラシイ。
English: Oooh... This is, this is... ...A superb example of a woman...

Japanese: ゼヒ一度、シンサツしてみたいの。 ん。もう、ゼヒ。
English: I'd like to... hmm, meet her... just one time...

Japanese: 明日、つれてきなさい。ん。 スゴい胃薬あげるから。
English: Bring her to me tomorrow. Hmm... She'll get special attention from me, I promise.

Japanese: （‥‥だいたい ここは外科だろ‥‥！）
English: (Wh-Where does he think he is!? This is a medical facility!!)

Japanese: この人なんですけど‥‥。
English: About this person...

Japanese: ‥‥むむむ。なんかこれは。 なんかこれは、どこかで。
English: Hmm... Hmm... I feel like... I feel like... Somewhere...

Japanese: 見たことがあるような。 ないような‥‥。むむ。
English: I've seen her somewhere before, I think... Hmm... Hmm...

Japanese: ま。それは明日、シンサツの ときにゆっくり。ひひひ。
English: Well, when she comes tomorrow for her consultation, I'm sure we can get re-acquainted...

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ‥‥）
English: (...I don't believe this...)

Japanese: お。ワシ。これ。ん。 イイね。この知的なハゲかた。
English: Oh, it's me. Hmm, yes... My tuft of hair... It's so nice. Shows off my smartness...

Japanese: キミもホレ。ワシめざして ハゲたまえ。ん。
English: You should try it too. My new hairstyle that I invented...

Japanese: ‥‥ココロがけます。
English: ...Ah, that's OK. I'll pass on this one...

Japanese: この人ですけど‥‥。
English: This person...

Japanese: お。いいね。この目。 “女王サマ”って感じ。
English: Ooh, she's nice. That look in her eye... She looks like a queen.

Japanese: こんな目でニラまれたらワシ。 もう。ホレ。むせび泣いちゃうな。
English: She's glaring at me. Hmm... Ah, her stare is making me choke up in tears.

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくなんか、ムチで たたかれましたよ。思いっきり。
English: ...I... I was whipped by her... And it hurt... a lot...

Japanese: うお！　ウラヤマシイ！
English: Oh, wow! I am so JEALOUS!

Japanese: どうだった？　もう、 泣いちゃったろ？　どうよその辺？
English: So? Did you cry? How did it feel?

Japanese: （‥‥ちょっと 泣いちゃったかも‥‥）
English: (...I cried a little... I think...? I can't actually remember...)

Japanese: この人なんですけど‥‥。
English: So, I'd like to ask you about this person...

Japanese: ‥‥ううむ。なんかね。こう、 グッと来ないな。
English: Hmm... Something tells me... Hmm, yes... Umm... No, nothing.

Japanese: シンサツする気になれん。 いいよ、つれて来なくて。
English: There is no need to bring this person in for an exam. Wouldn't do it anyway. Hmm...

Japanese: （だいたい、ジイさん、 医者じゃないじゃないか‥‥）
English: (Even as a pretend doctor, he gets an F from me...)

Japanese: （今はまだ、証拠が 足りないのかもしれない）
English: (I don't think I have enough evidence yet...)

Japanese: （もう少し調査をして、 材料を集めてみるか‥‥）
English: (I should investigate and gather some more clues before I try again...)

Japanese: （今はまだ、証拠が 足りないのかもしれない）
English: (I don't think I have enough evidence yet...)

Japanese: （もう少し調査をして、 材料を集めてみるか‥‥）
English: (I should investigate and gather some more clues before I try again...)

Japanese: ‥なるほどくん‥
English: ...Mr. Nick...

Japanese: ‥これ以上つづけると、 ココロがこわれてしまいます‥
English: ...If you push yourself any more, your soul will shatter...

Japanese: ‥もう一度、冷静になって 考えなおしましょう‥
English: ...Please calm down, collect your thoughts, and try again...

Japanese: （‥‥しまった！ シッパイしすぎたか‥‥）
English: (Nnrgh! I've made too many mistakes!)

Japanese: ‥‥ずっと気になっていたわ。
English: ...Something's been bothering me all this time.

Japanese: この犯行は、ふつうの人には 絶対にムリ。
English: This crime is something no normal person could carry out.

Japanese: 綾里屋敷のことや、倉院の里の しきたりを知っていなければ、ね。
English: The person would have to know a lot about Fey Manor and the village itself to do this.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、キミ子さんには アリバイがあった‥‥。
English: ...But Morgan already has an alibi...

Japanese: そう。だから、どうやったのか わからなかったわ。
English: Yes, which is why I can't figure out how it was done.

Japanese: のどかさんのことを 知るまでは‥‥。
English: We can assume that Ini Miney is involved somehow...

Japanese: でも‥‥１つだけ、 大きな問題がありますよ。
English: But we have one big problem on our hands.

Japanese: ‥‥問題？　何かしら？
English: ...A problem? With what?

Japanese: 動機です。
English: We don't have a motive.

Japanese: なぜキミ子さんが、霧崎先生を 殺害しなくちゃならないんですか？
English: Why would Morgan want to kill Dr. Grey? Why would she have to?

Japanese: それも、のどかさんと 協力してまで‥‥。
English: Also, we don't really know for sure that she was cooperating with Ini.

Japanese: （‥‥千尋さんも、 わかっていないのか‥‥）
English: (Mia doesn't have a clue either, huh?)

Japanese: ‥‥１つだけあるわ。 可能性が。
English: Hmm... Then, there is only one possibility...

Japanese: え！
English: Huh!?

Japanese: あなたも考えてみて。
English: Think about it, Phoenix.

Japanese: おばさまがなぜ、 こんな事件を起こしたのか？
English: Why would my aunt do something like this?

Japanese: しかも、こんな 手のこんだ方法を使って。
English: And why would she do it in such an underhanded fashion?
Um... because...?

Japanese: そのカギは‥‥、 母がにぎってるわ。
English: Mother... She is the one who holds the key to this.

Japanese: ‥‥おかあさん？ ぼくのですか？
English: Mother? Whose? Mine?

Japanese: 何をバカなこと言ってるの。 私の母よ。‥‥綾里　舞子。
English: Don't be silly. Mine, of course! Misty Fey.

Japanese: （千尋さんと真宵ちゃんの おかあさん‥‥？）
English: (Mia and Maya's mother? Why would she bring her up now...?)

Japanese: ‥‥さあ。
English: Now then...

Japanese: ついに、すべての手がかりが そろったわ。
English: Looks like we've gathered everything we could.

Japanese: ‥‥あとは、勝つだけ。 明日、すべてがあきらかになるわ。
English: All we have to do is win tomorrow! Everything will become apparent then.

Japanese: は‥‥はい！ よろしくおねがいします！
English: R-Right, Chief! We won't lose!

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.
